 //! SpendGuard stats_aggregator daemon entry point.
 //!
 //! Spec ref stats-aggregator-spec-v1alpha1.md §2.
 //!
 //! SLICE_06 boot sequence:
 //!
 //!   1. Install rustls aws_lc_rs crypto provider.
 //!   2. Load env config via [`spendguard_stats_aggregator::config::Config`].
 //!   3. Connect to canonical_ingest DB (read source + cache write target).
 //!   4. Connect to canonical_ingest gRPC for signed CloudEvent emission
 //!      (LoggingDriftAlertSink fallback when URL is empty — demo only;
 //!      production Helm gate enforces).
 //!   5. Spawn metrics hyper server (/metrics + /healthz + /readyz).
 //!   6. Run the scheduler loop (hourly per spec §8.1) until shutdown.
 //!
 //! Container security: USER 65532 + readOnlyRootFilesystem + cap_drop ALL
 //! enforced by the Helm chart (Phase F). The daemon itself has no
 //! privileged needs (no UDS bind, no socket bind besides the metrics TCP).
 
 use std::net::SocketAddr;
 use std::sync::Arc;
 use std::time::Duration;
 
 use anyhow::{Context, Result};
 use tonic::transport::{Certificate, ClientTlsConfig, Endpoint, Identity};
 use tracing::{error, info, warn};
 use tracing_subscriber::EnvFilter;
 
 use spendguard_signing::Signer;
 use spendguard_stats_aggregator::{
     aggregation::STATS_AGGREGATOR_ADVISORY_LOCK_ID,
     config::Config,
     drift_detector::{
-        CanonicalIngestDriftAlertSink, DriftAlertSink, DriftDetectorConfig, LoggingDriftAlertSink,
+        CanonicalIngestDriftAlertSink, DriftAlertCooldown, DriftAlertSink, DriftDetectorConfig,
+        LoggingDriftAlertSink, PostgresDriftAlertCooldownStore,
     },
     scheduler::run_loop,
 };
 
 #[tokio::main]
 async fn main() -> Result<()> {
     rustls::crypto::aws_lc_rs::default_provider()
         .install_default()
         .map_err(|_| anyhow::anyhow!("failed to install rustls aws_lc_rs default provider"))?;
 
     init_tracing();
 
     let cfg = Config::from_env().context("loading stats_aggregator config")?;
     info!(
         cycle_seconds = cfg.cycle_seconds,
         min_samples_for_alert = cfg.min_samples_for_alert,
         drift_z_threshold = cfg.drift_z_threshold,
         metrics_addr = %cfg.metrics_addr,
         region = %cfg.region,
         profile = %cfg.profile,
         canonical_ingest_url = %cfg.canonical_ingest_url,
         database_present = !cfg.database_url.is_empty(),
         advisory_lock_id = STATS_AGGREGATOR_ADVISORY_LOCK_ID,
         "starting spendguard-stats-aggregator"
     );
 
     // ── Validate config gates ─────────────────────────────────────
     if cfg.database_url.is_empty() {
         anyhow::bail!(
             "SPENDGUARD_STATS_AGGREGATOR_DATABASE_URL is required (canonical_ingest read+write source)"
         );
     }
     if cfg.cycle_seconds < 60 {
         warn!(
             cycle_seconds = cfg.cycle_seconds,
             "cycle_seconds < 60 — clamping to 60 to protect Postgres"
         );
     }
     let cycle_seconds = cfg.cycle_seconds.max(60);
 
     // ── Connect to canonical_ingest DB ────────────────────────────
     let pool = sqlx::postgres::PgPoolOptions::new()
         .max_connections(8)
         .acquire_timeout(Duration::from_secs(10))
         .connect(&cfg.database_url)
         .await
         .context("connect to canonical_ingest DB")?;
     info!("canonical_ingest DB pool connected");
 
     // ── Signer (audit-routed CloudEvent producer identity) ────────
     //
     // R2 M10: do NOT std::env::set_var() — that mutation is racy on
     // multi-threaded Rust (Rust 1.80+ marks set_var unsafe in code
     // running concurrently with other env reads). The signer crate's
     // signer_from_env reads `<prefix>_SIGNING_PRODUCER_IDENTITY`
     // directly; Helm sets this env var at pod startup (see
     // charts/spendguard/templates/stats_aggregator.yaml). No runtime
     // mutation required.
     let signer: Arc<dyn Signer> = Arc::from(
         spendguard_signing::signer_from_env("SPENDGUARD_STATS_AGGREGATOR")
             .await
             .context("load Ed25519 signer for prediction_drift_alert CloudEvent signing")?,
     );
     info!(
         producer_identity = %signer.producer_identity(),
         key_id = %signer.key_id(),
         "signer ready"
     );
 
     // ── Build drift alert sink ────────────────────────────────────
     //
     // R2 B5: production sink construction requires schema_bundle_id +
     // schema_bundle_hash_hex from config so the AppendEventsRequest
     // envelope carries the required fields. Demo profile may use the
     // LoggingDriftAlertSink which skips the envelope entirely.
     let sink: Arc<dyn DriftAlertSink> = if cfg.canonical_ingest_url.is_empty() {
         warn!(
             "SPENDGUARD_STATS_AGGREGATOR_CANONICAL_INGEST_URL not set — \
              drift_alert events will log to stdout (demo mode). \
              Production Helm profile rejects this fallback."
         );
         Arc::new(LoggingDriftAlertSink)
     } else {
         if cfg.schema_bundle_id.is_empty() {
             anyhow::bail!(
                 "SPENDGUARD_STATS_AGGREGATOR_SCHEMA_BUNDLE_ID required when \
                  SPENDGUARD_STATS_AGGREGATOR_CANONICAL_INGEST_URL is set \
                  (R2 B5: canonical_ingest rejects AppendEventsRequest \
                  without schema_bundle)"
             );
         }
         if cfg.schema_bundle_hash_hex.is_empty() {
             anyhow::bail!(
                 "SPENDGUARD_STATS_AGGREGATOR_SCHEMA_BUNDLE_HASH_HEX required \
                  when SPENDGUARD_STATS_AGGREGATOR_CANONICAL_INGEST_URL is set"
             );
         }
         let bundle_hash = hex::decode(&cfg.schema_bundle_hash_hex)
             .context("SPENDGUARD_STATS_AGGREGATOR_SCHEMA_BUNDLE_HASH_HEX must be hex-encoded")?;
         let schema_bundle_ref = spendguard_stats_aggregator::proto::common::v1::SchemaBundleRef {
             schema_bundle_id: cfg.schema_bundle_id.clone(),
             schema_bundle_hash: bundle_hash.into(),
             canonical_schema_version: cfg.canonical_schema_version.clone(),
         };
         let channel = build_canonical_ingest_channel(&cfg)
             .await
             .context("connect canonical_ingest channel")?;
         info!(
             url = %cfg.canonical_ingest_url,
             mtls = cfg.sink_tls_cert_pem.is_some(),
             schema_bundle_id = %cfg.schema_bundle_id,
             "canonical_ingest sink connected"
         );
         Arc::new(CanonicalIngestDriftAlertSink::new(
             channel,
             signer.producer_identity().to_string(),
             schema_bundle_ref,
             signer.key_id().to_string(),
         ))
     };
 
     let detector_cfg = DriftDetectorConfig {
         drift_z_threshold: cfg.drift_z_threshold,
         min_samples_for_alert: cfg.min_samples_for_alert,
     };
+    let cooldown: Arc<dyn DriftAlertCooldown> =
+        Arc::new(PostgresDriftAlertCooldownStore::new(pool.clone()));
 
     // ── Spawn metrics server ──────────────────────────────────────
     //
     // R2 M8: pass the DB pool + cycle-staleness threshold so /healthz +
     // /readyz can probe real subsystem state. /livez stays as pure
     // process liveness ("ok"). /readyz fails if no cycle has run in
     // 2× cycle_seconds (operator alert on stuck scheduler).
     if !cfg.metrics_addr.is_empty() {
         let addr: SocketAddr = cfg
             .metrics_addr
             .parse()
             .with_context(|| format!("invalid metrics_addr `{}`", cfg.metrics_addr))?;
         let pool_for_health = pool.clone();
         let max_cycle_age_secs = cycle_seconds.saturating_mul(2);
         tokio::spawn(async move {
             if let Err(e) = run_metrics_server(addr, pool_for_health, max_cycle_age_secs).await {
                 error!(?e, "metrics server exited with error");
             }
         });
         info!(
             addr = %cfg.metrics_addr,
             max_cycle_age_secs,
             "metrics endpoint bound"
         );
     }
 
     // ── Run scheduler loop (forever, until ctrl-c) ────────────────
     let pool_for_loop = pool.clone();
     let scheduler_handle = tokio::spawn(async move {
-        run_loop(pool_for_loop, cycle_seconds, detector_cfg, signer, sink).await;
+        run_loop(
+            pool_for_loop,
+            cycle_seconds,
+            detector_cfg,
+            signer,
+            sink,
+            cooldown,
+        )
+        .await;
     });
 
     shutdown_signal().await;
     scheduler_handle.abort();
     info!("spendguard-stats-aggregator shut down cleanly");
     Ok(())
 }
 
 /// Build the canonical_ingest gRPC channel with optional mTLS per spec
 /// §7.2 + tokenizer SLICE_05 R2 B4 convention.
 async fn build_canonical_ingest_channel(cfg: &Config) -> Result<tonic::transport::Channel> {
     let mut ep = Endpoint::from_shared(cfg.canonical_ingest_url.clone())
         .map_err(|e| anyhow::anyhow!("invalid canonical_ingest_url: {e}"))?
         .connect_timeout(Duration::from_secs(5))
         .timeout(Duration::from_secs(10))
         .keep_alive_timeout(Duration::from_secs(20))
         .keep_alive_while_idle(true);
 
     match (
         cfg.sink_tls_cert_pem.as_deref(),
         cfg.sink_tls_key_pem.as_deref(),
         cfg.sink_tls_ca_pem.as_deref(),
     ) {
         (None, None, None) => {
             warn!(
                 "canonical_ingest sink connecting WITHOUT mTLS — demo only; \
                  production Helm profile rejects this fallback."
             );
         }
         (Some(cert), Some(key), Some(ca)) => {
             let cert_pem = std::fs::read(cert).with_context(|| format!("read sink cert {cert}"))?;
             let key_pem = std::fs::read(key).with_context(|| format!("read sink key {key}"))?;
             let ca_pem = std::fs::read(ca).with_context(|| format!("read sink ca {ca}"))?;
             let tls = ClientTlsConfig::new()
                 .ca_certificate(Certificate::from_pem(ca_pem))
                 .identity(Identity::from_pem(cert_pem, key_pem))
                 .domain_name(&cfg.sink_tls_sni);
             ep = ep
                 .tls_config(tls)
                 .map_err(|e| anyhow::anyhow!("apply sink tls config: {e}"))?;
         }
         _ => {
             anyhow::bail!(
                 "partial sink mTLS config: must set all of sink_tls_cert_pem / sink_tls_key_pem / sink_tls_ca_pem, or none"
             );
         }
     }
     Ok(ep.connect_lazy())
 }
 
 fn init_tracing() {
     let env_filter = EnvFilter::try_from_default_env()
         .unwrap_or_else(|_| EnvFilter::new("spendguard_stats_aggregator=info,info"));
     tracing_subscriber::fmt()
         .with_env_filter(env_filter)
         .with_target(true)
         .json()
         .init();
 }
 
 async fn shutdown_signal() {
     let _ = tokio::signal::ctrl_c().await;
     info!("ctrl_c received — initiating graceful shutdown");
 }
 
 fn render_metrics() -> String {
     use spendguard_stats_aggregator::scheduler::{
-        CYCLES_TOTAL, CYCLE_ERROR_TOTAL, DRIFT_ALERTS_TOTAL, LAST_CYCLE_START_UNIX_SECS,
-        SKIPPED_LOCK_HELD_TOTAL,
+        CYCLES_TOTAL, CYCLE_ERROR_TOTAL, DRIFT_ALERTS_SUPPRESSED_TOTAL, DRIFT_ALERTS_TOTAL,
+        LAST_CYCLE_START_UNIX_SECS, SKIPPED_LOCK_HELD_TOTAL,
     };
     use std::sync::atomic::Ordering;
     // R2 M13: render the live AtomicU64 counters.
     format!(
         "# HELP spendguard_stats_aggregator_cycles_total \
          Total aggregation cycles attempted.\n\
          # TYPE spendguard_stats_aggregator_cycles_total counter\n\
          spendguard_stats_aggregator_cycles_total {}\n\
          # HELP spendguard_stats_aggregator_skipped_lock_held_total \
          Cycles skipped because the advisory lock was held by another instance.\n\
          # TYPE spendguard_stats_aggregator_skipped_lock_held_total counter\n\
          spendguard_stats_aggregator_skipped_lock_held_total {}\n\
          # HELP spendguard_stats_aggregator_drift_alerts_total \
          Total prediction_drift_alert CloudEvents emitted.\n\
          # TYPE spendguard_stats_aggregator_drift_alerts_total counter\n\
          spendguard_stats_aggregator_drift_alerts_total {}\n\
+         # HELP spendguard_stats_aggregator_drift_alerts_suppressed_total \
+         Total prediction_drift_alert CloudEvents suppressed by cooldown or numeric safety guards.\n\
+         # TYPE spendguard_stats_aggregator_drift_alerts_suppressed_total counter\n\
+         spendguard_stats_aggregator_drift_alerts_suppressed_total {}\n\
          # HELP spendguard_stats_aggregator_cycle_error_total \
          Total per-cycle errors (tenant aggregation failures + sink failures).\n\
          # TYPE spendguard_stats_aggregator_cycle_error_total counter\n\
          spendguard_stats_aggregator_cycle_error_total {}\n\
          # HELP spendguard_stats_aggregator_last_cycle_start_unix_secs \
          Unix timestamp (seconds) of the last cycle attempt; 0 if no cycle yet.\n\
          # TYPE spendguard_stats_aggregator_last_cycle_start_unix_secs gauge\n\
          spendguard_stats_aggregator_last_cycle_start_unix_secs {}\n",
         CYCLES_TOTAL.load(Ordering::Relaxed),
         SKIPPED_LOCK_HELD_TOTAL.load(Ordering::Relaxed),
         DRIFT_ALERTS_TOTAL.load(Ordering::Relaxed),
+        DRIFT_ALERTS_SUPPRESSED_TOTAL.load(Ordering::Relaxed),
         CYCLE_ERROR_TOTAL.load(Ordering::Relaxed),
         LAST_CYCLE_START_UNIX_SECS.load(Ordering::Relaxed),
     )
 }
 
 /// Minimal /metrics + /livez + /healthz + /readyz hyper server.
 ///
 /// R2 M8 (Security F8): real subsystem probes.
 ///   * /livez — pure process liveness, always 200 OK
 ///   * /healthz — DB pool ping (unhealthy if pool can't acquire)
 ///   * /readyz — DB pool ping AND cycle freshness (unready if last
 ///     cycle attempt > max_cycle_age_secs ago)
 async fn run_metrics_server(
     addr: SocketAddr,
     pool: sqlx::PgPool,
     max_cycle_age_secs: u64,
 ) -> Result<()> {
     use http_body_util::Full;
     use hyper::body::Bytes;
     use hyper::service::service_fn;
     use hyper::{Method, Request, Response, StatusCode};
     use hyper_util::rt::TokioIo;
     use spendguard_stats_aggregator::scheduler::LAST_CYCLE_START_UNIX_SECS;
     use std::sync::atomic::Ordering;
 
     let listener = tokio::net::TcpListener::bind(addr).await?;
     loop {
         let (stream, _) = listener.accept().await?;
         let io = TokioIo::new(stream);
         let pool_clone = pool.clone();
         tokio::spawn(async move {
             let svc = service_fn(move |req: Request<hyper::body::Incoming>| {
                 let pool = pool_clone.clone();
                 async move {
                     let (status, content_type, body): (StatusCode, &str, String) =
                         match (req.method(), req.uri().path()) {
                             (&Method::GET, "/metrics") => (
                                 StatusCode::OK,
                                 "text/plain; version=0.0.4; charset=utf-8",
                                 render_metrics(),
                             ),
                             (&Method::GET, "/livez") => (
                                 StatusCode::OK,
                                 "text/plain; charset=utf-8",
                                 "ok".to_string(),
                             ),
                             (&Method::GET, "/healthz") => {
                                 // R2 M8: DB pool ping. SELECT 1 covers
                                 // both connectivity + a trivial round-trip.
                                 match sqlx::query("SELECT 1").execute(&pool).await {
                                     Ok(_) => (
                                         StatusCode::OK,
                                         "text/plain; charset=utf-8",
                                         "ok".to_string(),
                                     ),
                                     Err(e) => (
                                         StatusCode::SERVICE_UNAVAILABLE,
                                         "text/plain; charset=utf-8",
                                         format!("db ping failed: {e}"),
                                     ),
                                 }
                             }
                             (&Method::GET, "/readyz") => {
                                 // R2 M8: DB ping + cycle freshness.
                                 let db_ok = sqlx::query("SELECT 1").execute(&pool).await.is_ok();
                                 let now_secs = std::time::SystemTime::now()
                                     .duration_since(std::time::UNIX_EPOCH)
                                     .map(|d| d.as_secs())
                                     .unwrap_or(0);
                                 let last_cycle = LAST_CYCLE_START_UNIX_SECS.load(Ordering::Relaxed);
                                 let cycle_fresh = last_cycle == 0
                                     || now_secs.saturating_sub(last_cycle) <= max_cycle_age_secs;
                                 if db_ok && cycle_fresh {
                                     (
                                         StatusCode::OK,
                                         "text/plain; charset=utf-8",
                                         "ready".to_string(),
                                     )
                                 } else {
                                     (
                                         StatusCode::SERVICE_UNAVAILABLE,
                                         "text/plain; charset=utf-8",
                                         format!(
                                             "not ready (db_ok={db_ok}, cycle_fresh={cycle_fresh}, \
                                              last_cycle={last_cycle}, now={now_secs}, \
                                              max_cycle_age_secs={max_cycle_age_secs})"
                                         ),
                                     )
                                 }
                             }
                             _ => (
                                 StatusCode::NOT_FOUND,
                                 "text/plain; charset=utf-8",
                                 "not found".to_string(),
                             ),
                         };
                     Ok::<_, std::convert::Infallible>(
                         Response::builder()
                             .status(status)
                             .header("content-type", content_type)
                             .body(Full::new(Bytes::from(body)))
                             .unwrap(),
                     )
                 }
             });
             if let Err(err) = hyper::server::conn::http1::Builder::new()
                 .serve_connection(io, svc)
                 .await
             {
                 error!(?err, "metrics conn error");
             }
         });
     }
 }
 
 #[cfg(test)]
 mod tests {
     use super::*;
 
     #[test]
     fn render_metrics_includes_known_names() {
         let body = render_metrics();
         assert!(body.contains("spendguard_stats_aggregator_cycles_total"));
         assert!(body.contains("spendguard_stats_aggregator_skipped_lock_held_total"));
         assert!(body.contains("spendguard_stats_aggregator_drift_alerts_total"));
+        assert!(body.contains("spendguard_stats_aggregator_drift_alerts_suppressed_total"));
     }
 }
diff --git a/services/stats_aggregator/src/scheduler.rs b/services/stats_aggregator/src/scheduler.rs
index cc59247..d6e78f4 100644
--- a/services/stats_aggregator/src/scheduler.rs
+++ b/services/stats_aggregator/src/scheduler.rs
@@ -1,200 +1,231 @@
 //! Cycle scheduler per spec stats-aggregator-spec-v1alpha1.md §8.
 //!
 //! ## Loop shape
 //!
 //! ```text
 //! loop {
 //!   sleep(cycle_seconds);
 //!   if try_acquire_lock() {
 //!     for tenant in discover_active_tenants():
 //!       aggregate_output_distribution(tenant)
 //!       aggregate_run_length(tenant)
 //!     detect_and_emit(aggregates)
 //!     release_lock()
 //!   } else {
 //!     metric: stats_aggregator_skipped_lock_held
 //!   }
 //! }
 //! ```
 //!
 //! Per spec §8.1 default cadence is hourly. Per-tenant cadence override
 //! (spec §8.2) is deferred to SLICE-extra; the scheduler currently
 //! treats every tenant uniformly.
 
 use std::sync::atomic::{AtomicU64, Ordering};
 use std::sync::Arc;
 use std::time::Duration;
 
 use anyhow::Context;
 use sqlx::postgres::PgPool;
 use tokio::time::interval;

     aggregate_output_distribution, discover_active_tenants, release_lock_conn,
     try_acquire_lock_conn,

 // R2 M13 (Software F15): real counters incremented from the cycle
 // loop. Rendered into the /metrics body. Exposed as `pub static` so
 // render_metrics in main.rs reads the live values without a global
 // registry.
 pub static CYCLES_TOTAL: AtomicU64 = AtomicU64::new(0);
 pub static SKIPPED_LOCK_HELD_TOTAL: AtomicU64 = AtomicU64::new(0);
 pub static DRIFT_ALERTS_TOTAL: AtomicU64 = AtomicU64::new(0);
+pub static DRIFT_ALERTS_SUPPRESSED_TOTAL: AtomicU64 = AtomicU64::new(0);
 pub static CYCLE_ERROR_TOTAL: AtomicU64 = AtomicU64::new(0);
 pub static LAST_CYCLE_START_UNIX_SECS: AtomicU64 = AtomicU64::new(0);
 
 /// One cycle's outcome surfaced for metric counters.
 #[derive(Debug, Clone)]
 pub struct CycleOutcome {
     pub lock_acquired: bool,
     pub tenants_processed: usize,
     pub alerts_emitted: usize,
+    pub alerts_suppressed: usize,
     pub error_count: usize,
 }
 
 /// Run one full aggregation cycle. Exposed separately from the loop so
 /// integration tests can invoke a single cycle.
 pub async fn run_one_cycle(
     pool: &PgPool,
     cfg: &DriftDetectorConfig,
     signer: Arc<dyn Signer>,
     sink: Arc<dyn DriftAlertSink>,
+    cooldown: Arc<dyn DriftAlertCooldown>,
 ) -> Result<CycleOutcome, anyhow::Error> {
     let mut outcome = CycleOutcome {
         lock_acquired: false,
         tenants_processed: 0,
         alerts_emitted: 0,
+        alerts_suppressed: 0,
         error_count: 0,
     };
 
     // R2 M13: stamp cycle-start so /healthz + /readyz can compute
     // last_cycle_age for staleness alerting (used by M8 wiring).
     let now_unix = std::time::SystemTime::now()
         .duration_since(std::time::UNIX_EPOCH)
         .map(|d| d.as_secs())
         .unwrap_or(0);
     LAST_CYCLE_START_UNIX_SECS.store(now_unix, Ordering::Relaxed);
     CYCLES_TOTAL.fetch_add(1, Ordering::Relaxed);
 
     // R2 B2: pin a PoolConnection for the entire cycle so the advisory
     // lock acquire + release run on the same session. Per-tenant
     // transactions are still checked out separately via pool.begin().
     let lock_conn = match try_acquire_lock_conn(pool).await.context("acquire lock")? {
         Some(c) => c,
         None => {
             SKIPPED_LOCK_HELD_TOTAL.fetch_add(1, Ordering::Relaxed);
             return Ok(outcome);
         }
     };
     outcome.lock_acquired = true;
 
     // Run the cycle body — capture errors so we can still release the
     // lock before returning. Per spec §8.3 the lock must release even
     // if any tenant aggregation panics (Postgres auto-releases on
     // session disconnect, but we want a clean release in normal flow).
-    let cycle_result = run_cycle_body(pool, cfg, signer, sink, &mut outcome).await;
+    let cycle_result = run_cycle_body(pool, cfg, signer, sink, cooldown, &mut outcome).await;
 
     if let Err(e) = release_lock_conn(lock_conn).await {
         warn!(error = %e, "release_lock failed (Postgres will auto-release on session disconnect)");
     }
 
     cycle_result?;
     Ok(outcome)
 }
 
 /// Inner cycle body — separated so the outer wrapper can release the
 /// advisory lock on both Ok + Err paths.
 async fn run_cycle_body(
     pool: &PgPool,
     cfg: &DriftDetectorConfig,
     signer: Arc<dyn Signer>,
     sink: Arc<dyn DriftAlertSink>,
+    cooldown: Arc<dyn DriftAlertCooldown>,
     outcome: &mut CycleOutcome,
 ) -> Result<(), anyhow::Error> {
     let tenants = match discover_active_tenants(pool).await {
         Ok(t) => t,
         Err(e) => {
             error!(error = %e, "discover_active_tenants failed");
             outcome.error_count += 1;
             return Ok(());
         }
     };
     info!(tenant_count = tenants.len(), "discovered active tenants");
 
     let mut all_aggregates = Vec::new();
     for tenant_id in tenants {
         match aggregate_output_distribution(pool, tenant_id).await {
             Ok(aggs) => {
                 outcome.tenants_processed += 1;
                 all_aggregates.extend(aggs);
             }
             Err(e) => {
                 warn!(tenant_id = %tenant_id, error = %e, "aggregate_output_distribution failed; other tenants continue");
                 outcome.error_count += 1;
             }
         }
         if let Err(e) = aggregate_run_length(pool, tenant_id).await {
             warn!(tenant_id = %tenant_id, error = %e, "aggregate_run_length failed; other tenants continue");
             outcome.error_count += 1;
         }
     }
 
-    match detect_and_emit(&all_aggregates, cfg, signer.as_ref(), sink.as_ref()).await {
-        Ok(n) => {
-            outcome.alerts_emitted = n;
+    match detect_and_emit(
+        &all_aggregates,
+        cfg,
+        signer.as_ref(),
+        sink.as_ref(),
+        cooldown.as_ref(),
+    )
+    .await
+    {
+        Ok(drift) => {
+            outcome.alerts_emitted = drift.emitted;
+            outcome.alerts_suppressed = drift.suppressed;
             // R2 M13: live counter for alert emission rate.
-            DRIFT_ALERTS_TOTAL.fetch_add(n as u64, Ordering::Relaxed);
-            info!(alerts = n, "drift detection complete");
+            DRIFT_ALERTS_TOTAL.fetch_add(drift.emitted as u64, Ordering::Relaxed);
+            DRIFT_ALERTS_SUPPRESSED_TOTAL.fetch_add(drift.suppressed as u64, Ordering::Relaxed);
+            info!(
+                alerts = drift.emitted,
+                suppressed = drift.suppressed,
+                "drift detection complete"
+            );
         }
         Err(e) => {
             error!(error = %e, "detect_and_emit failed; alerts may be lost");
             outcome.error_count += 1;
         }
     }
 
     // R2 M13: aggregate cycle-level error count → global counter
     // (gauges per-cycle errors via outcome; counter aggregates over the
     // process lifetime).
     if outcome.error_count > 0 {
         CYCLE_ERROR_TOTAL.fetch_add(outcome.error_count as u64, Ordering::Relaxed);
     }
 
     Ok(())
 }
 
 /// Long-lived scheduler loop. Returns only on graceful shutdown.
 pub async fn run_loop(
     pool: PgPool,
     cycle_seconds: u64,
     cfg: DriftDetectorConfig,
     signer: Arc<dyn Signer>,
     sink: Arc<dyn DriftAlertSink>,
+    cooldown: Arc<dyn DriftAlertCooldown>,
 ) {
     let mut ticker = interval(Duration::from_secs(cycle_seconds));
     // Tick once immediately so the first cycle runs at startup, then
     // wait `cycle_seconds` between subsequent ticks.
     ticker.set_missed_tick_behavior(tokio::time::MissedTickBehavior::Skip);
 
     loop {
         ticker.tick().await;
-        match run_one_cycle(&pool, &cfg, Arc::clone(&signer), Arc::clone(&sink)).await {
+        match run_one_cycle(
+            &pool,
+            &cfg,
+            Arc::clone(&signer),
+            Arc::clone(&sink),
+            Arc::clone(&cooldown),
+        )
+        .await
+        {
             Ok(outcome) => {
                 info!(
                     lock_acquired = outcome.lock_acquired,
                     tenants_processed = outcome.tenants_processed,
                     alerts_emitted = outcome.alerts_emitted,
+                    alerts_suppressed = outcome.alerts_suppressed,
                     error_count = outcome.error_count,
                     "cycle complete"
                 );
             }
             Err(e) => {
                 error!(error = %e, "run_one_cycle returned error");
             }
         }
     }
 }

     1	//! Drift detection + signed CloudEvent emission per spec
     2	//! stats-aggregator-spec-v1alpha1.md §7.
     3	//!
     4	//! ## Algorithm (spec §7.1)
     5	//!
     6	//! For each (tenant, model, agent_id, prompt_class) bucket whose 7d AND
     7	//! 30d windows both have data:
     8	//!
     9	//! ```text
    10	//! z_score = (mean_7d - mean_30d) / stddev_30d
    11	//!
    12	//! IF |z_score| > drift_z_threshold (default 2.0)
    13	//!    AND sample_size_7d >= MIN_SAMPLES_FOR_ALERT (default 100):
    14	//!   emit prediction_drift_alert CloudEvent
    15	//! ```
    16	//!
    17	//! The threshold is configurable per spec §0.3 GA prereq #1 ("per-tenant
    18	//! override"); SLICE_06 ships the global config; per-tenant override is
    19	//! SLICE-extra.
    20	//!
    21	//! ## Audit-routed CloudEvent
    22	//!
    23	//! Per SLICE_05 R2 B2 + tokenizer worker convention (worker.rs:72-81),
    24	//! drift_alert events use the `spendguard.audit.*` prefix to route into
    25	//! canonical_ingest's ImmutableAuditLog. The fallthrough
    26	//! `spendguard.prediction.*` (used in early drafts of the spec §7.2
    27	//! example YAML) would route to ProfilePayloadBlob which is
    28	//! RTBF-deletable and violates the spec §0.1 immutability claim.
    29

    46	/// + SLICE_05 R2 B2 audit-routed prefix discipline.

    48	    "spendguard.audit.prediction_drift_alert.v1alpha1";

    50	/// Configuration shared from main.rs into the drift detector. Pulled out
    51	/// of the daemon config so the detector module is unit-testable without
    52	/// the full envy + sqlx + tonic stack.
    53	#[derive(Debug, Clone)]
    54	pub struct DriftDetectorConfig {

    56	    pub min_samples_for_alert: i32,
    57	}

    59	pub const DRIFT_ALERT_COOLDOWN_HOURS: i64 = 24;

    61	#[derive(Debug, Clone, PartialEq, Eq, Hash)]

    63	    pub tenant_id: uuid::Uuid,
    64	    pub model: String,
    65	    pub agent_id: String,
    66	    pub prompt_class: String,
    67	}
    68	
    69	impl From<&BucketAggregate> for DriftAlertKey {
    70	    fn from(agg: &BucketAggregate) -> Self {
    71	        Self {
    72	            tenant_id: agg.tenant_id,
    73	            model: agg.model.clone(),
    74	            agent_id: agg.agent_id.clone(),
    75	            prompt_class: agg.prompt_class.clone(),
    76	        }
    77	    }
    78	}

    80	#[derive(Debug, Clone, PartialEq)]
    81	pub enum DriftAlertCooldownDecision {
    82	    Allowed { suppress_until: DateTime<Utc> },
    83	    Suppressed { suppress_until: DateTime<Utc> },
    84	}
    85	
    86	#[async_trait::async_trait]
    87	pub trait DriftAlertCooldown: Send + Sync {
    88	    async fn check(
    89	        &self,
    90	        key: &DriftAlertKey,
    91	        now: DateTime<Utc>,
    92	    ) -> Result<DriftAlertCooldownDecision, anyhow::Error>;
    93	
    94	    async fn record_emitted(
    95	        &self,
    96	        key: &DriftAlertKey,
    97	        emitted_at: DateTime<Utc>,
    98	        z_score: f32,
    99	    ) -> Result<DateTime<Utc>, anyhow::Error>;
   100	}
   101	
   102	pub struct PostgresDriftAlertCooldownStore {
   103	    pool: PgPool,
   104	}
   105	
   106	impl PostgresDriftAlertCooldownStore {
   107	    pub fn new(pool: PgPool) -> Self {
   108	        Self { pool }
   109	    }
   110	}
   111	
   112	#[async_trait::async_trait]
   113	impl DriftAlertCooldown for PostgresDriftAlertCooldownStore {
   114	    async fn check(
   115	        &self,
   116	        key: &DriftAlertKey,
   117	        now: DateTime<Utc>,
   118	    ) -> Result<DriftAlertCooldownDecision, anyhow::Error> {
   119	        let mut tx = self.pool.begin().await.context("begin drift cooldown tx")?;
   120	        sqlx::query("SELECT set_config('app.current_tenant_id', $1, true)")
   121	            .bind(key.tenant_id.to_string())
   122	            .execute(&mut *tx)
   123	            .await
   124	            .context("set RLS tenant_id for prediction_drift_alert_cooldowns")?;
   125	
   126	        let active_until = sqlx::query_scalar::<_, DateTime<Utc>>(
   127	            r#"
   128	            SELECT suppress_until
   129	            FROM prediction_drift_alert_cooldowns
   130	            WHERE tenant_id = $1
   131	              AND model = $2
   132	              AND agent_id = $3
   133	              AND prompt_class = $4
   134	            "#,
   135	        )
   136	        .bind(key.tenant_id)
   137	        .bind(&key.model)
   138	        .bind(&key.agent_id)
   139	        .bind(&key.prompt_class)
   140	        .fetch_optional(&mut *tx)
   141	        .await
   142	        .context("read prediction_drift_alert cooldown")?;
   143	
   144	        let decision = match active_until {
   145	            Some(until) if until > now => DriftAlertCooldownDecision::Suppressed {
   146	                suppress_until: until,
   147	            },
   148	            _ => DriftAlertCooldownDecision::Allowed {
   149	                suppress_until: now + ChronoDuration::hours(DRIFT_ALERT_COOLDOWN_HOURS),
   150	            },
   151	        };
   152	
   153	        tx.commit()
   154	            .await
   155	            .context("commit drift cooldown check tx")?;
   156	        Ok(decision)
   157	    }
   158	
   159	    async fn record_emitted(
   160	        &self,
   161	        key: &DriftAlertKey,
   162	        emitted_at: DateTime<Utc>,
   163	        z_score: f32,
   164	    ) -> Result<DateTime<Utc>, anyhow::Error> {
   165	        if !z_score.is_finite() {
   166	            anyhow::bail!("non-finite z_score cannot enter drift alert cooldown store");
   167	        }
   168	
   169	        let suppress_until = emitted_at + ChronoDuration::hours(DRIFT_ALERT_COOLDOWN_HOURS);
   170	        let mut tx = self.pool.begin().await.context("begin drift cooldown tx")?;
   171	        sqlx::query("SELECT set_config('app.current_tenant_id', $1, true)")
   172	            .bind(key.tenant_id.to_string())
   173	            .execute(&mut *tx)
   174	            .await
   175	            .context("set RLS tenant_id for prediction_drift_alert_cooldowns")?;
   176	
   177	        let recorded_until = sqlx::query_scalar::<_, DateTime<Utc>>(
   178	            r#"
   179	            INSERT INTO prediction_drift_alert_cooldowns (
   180	              tenant_id, model, agent_id, prompt_class,
   181	              last_emitted_at, suppress_until, last_z_score, updated_at
   182	            )
   183	            VALUES ($1, $2, $3, $4, $5, $6, $7, clock_timestamp())
   184	            ON CONFLICT (tenant_id, model, agent_id, prompt_class)
   185	              DO UPDATE SET
   186	                last_emitted_at = EXCLUDED.last_emitted_at,
   187	                suppress_until = EXCLUDED.suppress_until,
   188	                last_z_score = EXCLUDED.last_z_score,
   189	                updated_at = clock_timestamp()
   190	            RETURNING suppress_until
   191	            "#,
   192	        )
   193	        .bind(key.tenant_id)
   194	        .bind(&key.model)
   195	        .bind(&key.agent_id)
   196	        .bind(&key.prompt_class)
   197	        .bind(emitted_at)
   198	        .bind(suppress_until)
   199	        .bind(z_score)
   200	        .fetch_one(&mut *tx)
   201	        .await
   202	        .context("record prediction_drift_alert cooldown")?;
   203	
   204	        tx.commit().await.context("commit drift cooldown tx")?;
   205	        Ok(recorded_until)
   206	    }
   207	}
   208	
   209	/// Per spec §7.3 suggested action heuristic.
   210	fn suggest_action(z_score: f32) -> &'static str {
   211	    if z_score > 0.0 {
   212	        "investigate_agent_change"
   213	    } else {
   214	        "review_predictor_baseline"
   215	    }
   216	}
   217	
   218	/// Compute z-score per spec §7.1.
   219	///
   220	/// R2 M2 (Software F5): baseline EXCLUDES the current 7-day window.
   221	/// R1 shape used mean_30d (which includes the last 7d) → baseline was
   222	/// contaminated by the very window we were comparing against, biasing
   223	/// z toward 0. Now we read baseline_mean / baseline_stddev which were
   224	/// computed over [now - 30d, now - 7d] (see aggregation.rs::agg_baseline).
   225	///
   226	/// Returns None when:
   227	///   * 7d window is missing (no current sample to compare)
   228	///   * baseline window is missing (new bucket; first activity in 7d)
   229	///   * baseline_stddev == 0 (all baseline samples identical → undefined ratio)
   230	pub fn compute_z_score(agg: &BucketAggregate) -> Option<f32> {
   231	    let mean_7d = agg.mean_7d?;
   232	    let baseline_mean = agg.baseline_mean?;
   233	    let baseline_stddev = agg.baseline_stddev?;
   234	    if !mean_7d.is_finite() || !baseline_mean.is_finite() || !baseline_stddev.is_finite() {
   235	        return None;
   236	    }
   237	    if baseline_stddev <= 0.0 {
   238	        // baseline_stddev == 0 → all baseline samples identical.
   239	        // Strict spec interpretation: z-score is undefined → no alert.
   240	        return None;
   241	    }
   242	    let z = (mean_7d - baseline_mean) / baseline_stddev;
   243	    z.is_finite().then_some(z)
   244	}
   245	
   246	/// Decision predicate: should this bucket emit a drift alert?
   247	pub fn should_emit_drift_alert(agg: &BucketAggregate, cfg: &DriftDetectorConfig) -> bool {
   248	    if !cfg.drift_z_threshold.is_finite() || cfg.drift_z_threshold <= 0.0 {
   249	        return false;
   250	    }
   251	    let Some(z) = compute_z_score(agg) else {
   252	        return false;
   253	    };
   254	    let Some(n7d) = agg.sample_size_7d else {
   255	        return false;
   256	    };
   257	    z.abs() > cfg.drift_z_threshold && n7d >= cfg.min_samples_for_alert
   258	}
   259	
   260	/// Build (but do not sign or emit) the prediction_drift_alert

     1	//! Cycle scheduler per spec stats-aggregator-spec-v1alpha1.md §8.
     2	//!
     3	//! ## Loop shape
     4	//!
     5	//! ```text
     6	//! loop {
     7	//!   sleep(cycle_seconds);
     8	//!   if try_acquire_lock() {
     9	//!     for tenant in discover_active_tenants():
    10	//!       aggregate_output_distribution(tenant)
    11	//!       aggregate_run_length(tenant)
    12	//!     detect_and_emit(aggregates)
    13	//!     release_lock()
    14	//!   } else {
    15	//!     metric: stats_aggregator_skipped_lock_held
    16	//!   }
    17	//! }
    18	//! ```
    19	//!
    20	//! Per spec §8.1 default cadence is hourly. Per-tenant cadence override
    21	//! (spec §8.2) is deferred to SLICE-extra; the scheduler currently
    22	//! treats every tenant uniformly.

    27

    64	/// Run one full aggregation cycle. Exposed separately from the loop so

    66	pub async fn run_one_cycle(
    67	    pool: &PgPool,
    68	    cfg: &DriftDetectorConfig,
    69	    signer: Arc<dyn Signer>,
    70	    sink: Arc<dyn DriftAlertSink>,

    74	        lock_acquired: false,
    75	        tenants_processed: 0,

    77	        alerts_suppressed: 0,
    78	        error_count: 0,
    79	    };
    80	
    81	    // R2 M13: stamp cycle-start so /healthz + /readyz can compute
    82	    // last_cycle_age for staleness alerting (used by M8 wiring).
    83	    let now_unix = std::time::SystemTime::now()
    84	        .duration_since(std::time::UNIX_EPOCH)
    85	        .map(|d| d.as_secs())
    86	        .unwrap_or(0);
    87	    LAST_CYCLE_START_UNIX_SECS.store(now_unix, Ordering::Relaxed);
    88	    CYCLES_TOTAL.fetch_add(1, Ordering::Relaxed);
    89	
    90	    // R2 B2: pin a PoolConnection for the entire cycle so the advisory
    91	    // lock acquire + release run on the same session. Per-tenant
    92	    // transactions are still checked out separately via pool.begin().
    93	    let lock_conn = match try_acquire_lock_conn(pool).await.context("acquire lock")? {
    94	        Some(c) => c,
    95	        None => {
    96	            SKIPPED_LOCK_HELD_TOTAL.fetch_add(1, Ordering::Relaxed);
    97	            return Ok(outcome);
    98	        }
    99	    };
   100	    outcome.lock_acquired = true;
   101	
   102	    // Run the cycle body — capture errors so we can still release the
   103	    // lock before returning. Per spec §8.3 the lock must release even
   104	    // if any tenant aggregation panics (Postgres auto-releases on
   105	    // session disconnect, but we want a clean release in normal flow).
   106	    let cycle_result = run_cycle_body(pool, cfg, signer, sink, cooldown, &mut outcome).await;
   107	
   108	    if let Err(e) = release_lock_conn(lock_conn).await {
   109	        warn!(error = %e, "release_lock failed (Postgres will auto-release on session disconnect)");
   110	    }
   111	
   112	    cycle_result?;
   113	    Ok(outcome)
   114	}
   115	
   116	/// Inner cycle body — separated so the outer wrapper can release the
   117	/// advisory lock on both Ok + Err paths.
   118	async fn run_cycle_body(
   119	    pool: &PgPool,
   120	    cfg: &DriftDetectorConfig,
   121	    signer: Arc<dyn Signer>,
   122	    sink: Arc<dyn DriftAlertSink>,
   123	    cooldown: Arc<dyn DriftAlertCooldown>,
   124	    outcome: &mut CycleOutcome,
   125	) -> Result<(), anyhow::Error> {
   126	    let tenants = match discover_active_tenants(pool).await {
   127	        Ok(t) => t,
   128	        Err(e) => {
   129	            error!(error = %e, "discover_active_tenants failed");
   130	            outcome.error_count += 1;
   131	            return Ok(());
   132	        }
   133	    };
   134	    info!(tenant_count = tenants.len(), "discovered active tenants");
   135	
   136	    let mut all_aggregates = Vec::new();
   137	    for tenant_id in tenants {
   138	        match aggregate_output_distribution(pool, tenant_id).await {
   139	            Ok(aggs) => {
   140	                outcome.tenants_processed += 1;
   141	                all_aggregates.extend(aggs);
   142	            }
   143	            Err(e) => {
   144	                warn!(tenant_id = %tenant_id, error = %e, "aggregate_output_distribution failed; other tenants continue");
   145	                outcome.error_count += 1;
   146	            }
   147	        }
   148	        if let Err(e) = aggregate_run_length(pool, tenant_id).await {
   149	            warn!(tenant_id = %tenant_id, error = %e, "aggregate_run_length failed; other tenants continue");
   150	            outcome.error_count += 1;
   151	        }
   152	    }
   153	
   154	    match detect_and_emit(
   155	        &all_aggregates,
   156	        cfg,
   157	        signer.as_ref(),
   158	        sink.as_ref(),
   159	        cooldown.as_ref(),
   160	    )
   161	    .await
   162	    {
   163	        Ok(drift) => {
   164	            outcome.alerts_emitted = drift.emitted;
   165	            outcome.alerts_suppressed = drift.suppressed;
   166	            // R2 M13: live counter for alert emission rate.
   167	            DRIFT_ALERTS_TOTAL.fetch_add(drift.emitted as u64, Ordering::Relaxed);
   168	            DRIFT_ALERTS_SUPPRESSED_TOTAL.fetch_add(drift.suppressed as u64, Ordering::Relaxed);
   169	            info!(
   170	                alerts = drift.emitted,
   171	                suppressed = drift.suppressed,
   172	                "drift detection complete"
   173	            );
   174	        }
   175	        Err(e) => {
   176	            error!(error = %e, "detect_and_emit failed; alerts may be lost");
   177	            outcome.error_count += 1;
   178	        }
   179	    }
   180	
   181	    // R2 M13: aggregate cycle-level error count → global counter
   182	    // (gauges per-cycle errors via outcome; counter aggregates over the
   183	    // process lifetime).
   184	    if outcome.error_count > 0 {
   185	        CYCLE_ERROR_TOTAL.fetch_add(outcome.error_count as u64, Ordering::Relaxed);
   186	    }
   187	
   188	    Ok(())
   189	}
   190	
   191	/// Long-lived scheduler loop. Returns only on graceful shutdown.
   192	pub async fn run_loop(
   193	    pool: PgPool,
   194	    cycle_seconds: u64,
   195	    cfg: DriftDetectorConfig,
   196	    signer: Arc<dyn Signer>,
   197	    sink: Arc<dyn DriftAlertSink>,
   198	    cooldown: Arc<dyn DriftAlertCooldown>,
   199	) {
   200	    let mut ticker = interval(Duration::from_secs(cycle_seconds));
   201	    // Tick once immediately so the first cycle runs at startup, then
   202	    // wait `cycle_seconds` between subsequent ticks.
   203	    ticker.set_missed_tick_behavior(tokio::time::MissedTickBehavior::Skip);
   204	
   205	    loop {
   206	        ticker.tick().await;
   207	        match run_one_cycle(
   208	            &pool,
   209	            &cfg,
   210	            Arc::clone(&signer),
   211	            Arc::clone(&sink),
   212	            Arc::clone(&cooldown),
   213	        )
   214	        .await
   215	        {
   216	            Ok(outcome) => {
   217	                info!(
   218	                    lock_acquired = outcome.lock_acquired,
   219	                    tenants_processed = outcome.tenants_processed,
   220	                    alerts_emitted = outcome.alerts_emitted,
   221	                    alerts_suppressed = outcome.alerts_suppressed,
   222	                    error_count = outcome.error_count,
   223	                    "cycle complete"
   224	                );
   225	            }
   226	            Err(e) => {
   227	                error!(error = %e, "run_one_cycle returned error");
   228	            }
   229	        }
   230	    }
   231	}

     2	-- 0022_prediction_drift_alert_cooldowns.sql — POST_GA_06 drift alert dedup.
     3	--
     4	-- Issues:
     5	--   - #157: 24h cooldown/dedup per (tenant, model, agent_id, prompt_class)
     6	--   - #162: keep non-finite z-scores out of alert state/payloads
     7	--
     8	-- This is mutable derived state owned by stats_aggregator. It gates
     9	-- prediction_drift_alert emission before a new immutable audit event is sent
    10	-- to canonical_ingest, so repeated drift in the same bucket does not spam the
    11	-- audit chain. It deliberately lives beside canonical_events because the
    12	-- stats_aggregator already connects to the canonical DB for aggregation state.
    13	-- ============================================================================
    14	
    15	CREATE TABLE prediction_drift_alert_cooldowns (
    16	    tenant_id       UUID        NOT NULL,
    17	    model           TEXT        NOT NULL CHECK (char_length(model) BETWEEN 1 AND 64),
    18	    agent_id        TEXT        NOT NULL CHECK (char_length(agent_id) BETWEEN 1 AND 128),
    19	    prompt_class    TEXT        NOT NULL CHECK (prompt_class IN (
    20	        'chat_short', 'chat_long', 'code_gen', 'summarization',
    21	        'rag', 'tool_calling', 'vision'
    22	    )),
    23	    last_emitted_at TIMESTAMPTZ NOT NULL,
    24	    suppress_until  TIMESTAMPTZ NOT NULL,
    25	    last_z_score    REAL        NOT NULL CHECK (
    26	        last_z_score <> 'NaN'::REAL
    27	        AND last_z_score <> 'Infinity'::REAL
    28	        AND last_z_score <> '-Infinity'::REAL
    29	    ),
    30	    updated_at      TIMESTAMPTZ NOT NULL DEFAULT clock_timestamp(),
    31	
    32	    PRIMARY KEY (tenant_id, model, agent_id, prompt_class),
    33	    CHECK (suppress_until > last_emitted_at)
    34	);

   260	/// Build (but do not sign or emit) the prediction_drift_alert
   261	/// CloudEvent for a bucket. Separated from the emission step so unit
   262	/// tests can verify the envelope shape without a tonic Channel.
   263	pub fn build_drift_alert(
   264	    agg: &BucketAggregate,
   265	    z_score: f32,
   266	    cfg: &DriftDetectorConfig,
   267	) -> Option<CloudEvent> {
   268	    use bytes::Bytes;
   269	    if !drift_alert_payload_is_finite(agg, z_score, cfg) {
   270	        return None;
   271	    }
   272	    let now = Utc::now();
   273	    // R2 M2: payload reports baseline_* fields (window [now-30d, now-7d]
   274	    // — distinct from mean_30d which is the strategy-B cache baseline).
   275	    // Calibration-report consumers want both for trend analysis.
   276	    let data = serde_json::json!({
   277	        "tenant_id": agg.tenant_id.to_string(),
   278	        "model": agg.model,
   279	        "agent_id": agg.agent_id,
   280	        "prompt_class": agg.prompt_class,
   281	        "baseline_mean": agg.baseline_mean,
   282	        "baseline_stddev": agg.baseline_stddev,
   283	        "baseline_sample_size": agg.baseline_sample_size,
   284	        "current_mean_7d": agg.mean_7d,
   285	        "z_score": z_score,
   286	        "sample_size_7d": agg.sample_size_7d,
   287	        "sample_size_30d": agg.sample_size_30d,
   288	        "drift_z_threshold": cfg.drift_z_threshold,
   289	        "min_samples_for_alert": cfg.min_samples_for_alert,
   290	        "suggested_action": suggest_action(z_score),
   291	    });
   292	    let data_bytes = serde_json::to_vec(&data).unwrap_or_default();
   293	
   294	    Some(CloudEvent {
   295	        specversion: "1.0".to_string(),
   296	        r#type: PREDICTION_DRIFT_ALERT_EVENT_TYPE.to_string(),
   297	        source: format!("spendguard://stats-aggregator/{}", agg.tenant_id),
   298	        id: uuid::Uuid::now_v7().to_string(),
   299	        time: Some(prost_types::Timestamp {
   300	            seconds: now.timestamp(),
   301	            nanos: now.timestamp_subsec_nanos() as i32,
   302	        }),
   303	        datacontenttype: "application/json".to_string(),
   304	        data: data_bytes.into(),
   305	        tenant_id: agg.tenant_id.to_string(),
   306	        producer_signature: Bytes::new(),
   307	        ..Default::default()
   308	    })
   309	}
   310	
   311	fn drift_alert_payload_is_finite(
   312	    agg: &BucketAggregate,
   313	    z_score: f32,
   314	    cfg: &DriftDetectorConfig,
   315	) -> bool {
   316	    z_score.is_finite()
   317	        && cfg.drift_z_threshold.is_finite()
   318	        && cfg.drift_z_threshold > 0.0
   319	        && agg.baseline_mean.is_some_and(f32::is_finite)
   320	        && agg
   321	            .baseline_stddev
   322	            .is_some_and(|stddev| stddev.is_finite() && stddev > 0.0)
   323	        && agg.mean_7d.is_some_and(f32::is_finite)
   324	}

   326	/// Sink trait — abstracts the actual canonical_ingest gRPC call so
   327	/// tests can swap in an in-memory recorder.
   328	#[async_trait::async_trait]
   329	pub trait DriftAlertSink: Send + Sync {
   330	    async fn emit(&self, event: CloudEvent) -> Result<(), anyhow::Error>;
   331	}
   332	
   333	/// Logging-only sink for demo / development. Logs the event at INFO
   334	/// level instead of sending to canonical_ingest. Production Helm gate
   335	/// rejects this path when chart.profile=production.
   336	pub struct LoggingDriftAlertSink;
   337	
   338	#[async_trait::async_trait]
   339	impl DriftAlertSink for LoggingDriftAlertSink {
   340	    async fn emit(&self, event: CloudEvent) -> Result<(), anyhow::Error> {
   341	        info!(
   342	            event_type = %event.r#type,
   343	            event_id = %event.id,
   344	            tenant_id = %event.tenant_id,
   345	            "drift_alert emitted (log sink — demo only)"
   346	        );
   347	        Ok(())
   348	    }
   349	}
   350	
   351	/// canonical_ingest sink emitting one AppendEventsRequest per alert.
   352	///
   353	/// R2 B5: AppendEventsRequest envelope MUST carry producer_id +
   354	/// schema_bundle + route or canonical_ingest's append handler rejects
   355	/// the call with `producer_id required` / `schema_bundle required` /
   356	/// `route is unspecified` (see
   357	/// services/canonical_ingest/src/handlers/append_events.rs:64,73,101).
   358	/// Defaults from R1 shape (`..Default::default()`) failed all three.
   359	pub struct CanonicalIngestDriftAlertSink {
   360	    client: tokio::sync::Mutex<CanonicalIngestClient<Channel>>,
   361	    producer_id: String,
   362	    schema_bundle_ref: SchemaBundleRef,
   363	    signing_key_id: String,
   364	}
   365	
   366	impl CanonicalIngestDriftAlertSink {
   367	    /// Build the sink with the envelope fields the canonical_ingest
   368	    /// handler requires.
   369	    ///
   370	    /// * `producer_id` — typically `stats-aggregator:<region>`; matches
   371	    ///   the `signer.producer_identity()` discipline used by tokenizer
   372	    ///   SLICE_05.
   373	    /// * `schema_bundle_ref` — pre-built SchemaBundleRef (id + hash +
   374	    ///   canonical_schema_version). Built in main.rs from config + the
   375	    ///   hex-decoded hash bytes (mirror of outbox_forwarder).
   376	    /// * `signing_key_id` — the AppendEventsRequest batch-level
   377	    ///   signing_key_id (transport check only; per-event signature is on
   378	    ///   each CloudEvent). Reuse signer.key_id() for consistency.
   379	    pub fn new(
   380	        channel: Channel,
   381	        producer_id: String,
   382	        schema_bundle_ref: SchemaBundleRef,
   383	        signing_key_id: String,
   384	    ) -> Self {
   385	        Self {
   386	            client: tokio::sync::Mutex::new(CanonicalIngestClient::new(channel)),
   387	            producer_id,
   388	            schema_bundle_ref,
   389	            signing_key_id,
   390	        }
   391	    }
   392	}
   393	
   394	#[async_trait::async_trait]
   395	impl DriftAlertSink for CanonicalIngestDriftAlertSink {
   396	    async fn emit(&self, event: CloudEvent) -> Result<(), anyhow::Error> {
   397	        let req = AppendEventsRequest {
   398	            producer_id: self.producer_id.clone(),
   399	            batch_max_producer_sequence: 0,
   400	            // batch_signature optional per Trace §13 — per-event
   401	            // signature on each CloudEvent is the canonical truth; we
   402	            // leave batch_signature empty (allowed in-cluster on mTLS).
   403	            batch_signature: bytes::Bytes::new(),
   404	            signing_key_id: self.signing_key_id.clone(),
   405	            schema_bundle: Some(self.schema_bundle_ref.clone()),
   406	            events: vec![event],
   407	            // OBSERVABILITY: drift_alert is an observability event, not
   408	            // an enforcement event. canonical_ingest's failure mode for
   409	            // OBSERVABILITY is buffer_then_retry (per Trace §10.1).
   410	            route: Route::Observability as i32,
   411	        };
   412	        let mut guard = self.client.lock().await;
   413	        let resp = guard
   414	            .append_events(req)
   415	            .await
   416	            .context("canonical_ingest AppendEvents")?;
   417	        ensure_append_accepted(resp.into_inner())
   418	    }
   419	}
   420	
   421	pub(crate) fn ensure_append_accepted(resp: AppendEventsResponse) -> Result<(), anyhow::Error> {
   422	    if resp.results.len() != 1 {
   423	        return Err(anyhow::anyhow!(
   424	            "AppendEvents prediction_drift_alert returned {} results for one event",
   425	            resp.results.len()
   426	        ));
   427	    }
   428	
   429	    let result = &resp.results[0];
   430	    let status = EventStatus::try_from(result.status).unwrap_or(EventStatus::Unspecified);
   431	    match status {
   432	        EventStatus::Appended | EventStatus::Deduped => Ok(()),
   433	        other => {
   434	            let error_message = result
   435	                .error
   436	                .as_ref()
   437	                .map(|err| err.message.as_str())
   438	                .filter(|msg| !msg.is_empty())
   439	                .unwrap_or("canonical_ingest returned no error detail");
   440	            warn!(
   441	                event_id = %result.event_id,
   442	                status = ?other,
   443	                err = %error_message,
   444	                "canonical_ingest rejected prediction_drift_alert"
   445	            );
   446	            Err(anyhow::anyhow!(
   447	                "AppendEvents prediction_drift_alert rejected event_id={} status={:?}: {}",
   448	                result.event_id,
   449	                other,
   450	                error_message
   451	            ))
   452	        }
   453	    }
   454	}
   455	
   456	/// Detect drift across a batch of bucket aggregates and emit signed
   457	/// CloudEvents for those exceeding the threshold. Returns the count of
   458	/// alerts emitted for metrics.
   459	#[derive(Debug, Clone, Copy, Default, PartialEq, Eq)]
   460	pub struct DriftDetectionOutcome {
   461	    pub emitted: usize,
   462	    pub suppressed: usize,
   463	}
   464	
   465	pub async fn detect_and_emit(
   466	    aggregates: &[BucketAggregate],
   467	    cfg: &DriftDetectorConfig,
   468	    signer: &dyn Signer,
   469	    sink: &dyn DriftAlertSink,
   470	    cooldown: &dyn DriftAlertCooldown,
   471	) -> Result<DriftDetectionOutcome, anyhow::Error> {
   472	    let mut outcome = DriftDetectionOutcome::default();
   473	    for agg in aggregates {
   474	        if !should_emit_drift_alert(agg, cfg) {
   475	            continue;
   476	        }
   477	        let z = match compute_z_score(agg) {
   478	            Some(z) => z,
   479	            None => continue,
   480	        };
   481	        let key = DriftAlertKey::from(agg);
   482	        match cooldown.check(&key, Utc::now()).await {
   483	            Ok(DriftAlertCooldownDecision::Allowed { .. }) => {}
   484	            Ok(DriftAlertCooldownDecision::Suppressed { suppress_until }) => {
   485	                outcome.suppressed += 1;
   486	                info!(
   487	                    tenant_id = %agg.tenant_id,
   488	                    model = %agg.model,
   489	                    agent_id = %agg.agent_id,
   490	                    prompt_class = %agg.prompt_class,
   491	                    suppress_until = %suppress_until,
   492	                    "drift alert suppressed by cooldown"
   493	                );
   494	                continue;
   495	            }
   496	            Err(e) => {
   497	                outcome.suppressed += 1;
   498	                warn!(
   499	                    tenant_id = %agg.tenant_id,
   500	                    model = %agg.model,
   501	                    agent_id = %agg.agent_id,
   502	                    prompt_class = %agg.prompt_class,
   503	                    error = %e,
   504	                    "drift alert cooldown unavailable; suppressing alert to avoid immutable audit spam"
   505	                );
   506	                continue;
   507	            }
   508	        }
   509	        let Some(mut ce) = build_drift_alert(agg, z, cfg) else {
   510	            outcome.suppressed += 1;
   511	            warn!(
   512	                tenant_id = %agg.tenant_id,
   513	                model = %agg.model,
   514	                agent_id = %agg.agent_id,
   515	                prompt_class = %agg.prompt_class,
   516	                z_score = z,
   517	                "drift alert payload had non-finite numeric field; suppressing alert"
   518	            );
   519	            continue;
   520	        };
   521	        // Sign the canonical bytes (matches tokenizer worker.rs
   522	        // sign_in_place pattern).
   523	        ce.signing_key_id = signer.key_id().to_string();
   524	        ce.producer_id = signer.producer_identity().to_string();
   525	        ce.producer_signature = bytes::Bytes::new();
   526	        let canonical = ce.encode_to_vec();
   527	        let sig = signer
   528	            .sign(&canonical)
   529	            .await
   530	            .map_err(|e| anyhow::anyhow!("sign drift_alert CloudEvent: {e}"))?;
   531	        ce.producer_signature = sig.bytes.into();
   532	
   533	        match sink.emit(ce).await {
   534	            Ok(()) => {
   535	                outcome.emitted += 1;
   536	                if let Err(e) = cooldown.record_emitted(&key, Utc::now(), z).await {
   537	                    warn!(
   538	                        tenant_id = %agg.tenant_id,
   539	                        model = %agg.model,
   540	                        agent_id = %agg.agent_id,
   541	                        prompt_class = %agg.prompt_class,
   542	                        error = %e,
   543	                        "drift alert emitted but cooldown record failed; future duplicate suppression may be unavailable"
   544	                    );
   545	                }
   546	                debug!(
   547	                    tenant_id = %agg.tenant_id,
   548	                    model = %agg.model,
   549	                    agent_id = %agg.agent_id,
   550	                    prompt_class = %agg.prompt_class,
   551	                    z_score = z,
   552	                    "drift alert emitted"
   553	                );
   554	            }
   555	            Err(e) => {
   556	                warn!(
   557	                    tenant_id = %agg.tenant_id,
   558	                    error = %e,
   559	                    "drift alert emit failed before cooldown record; will retry next cycle"
   560	                );

exec
/bin/zsh -lc "nl -ba services/stats_aggregator/src/drift_detector.rs | sed -n '560,1120p'" in /Users/michael.chen/products/agentic-spendguard
 succeeded in 0ms:
   560	                );
   561	            }
   562	        }
   563	    }
   564	    Ok(outcome)
   565	}
   566	
   567	#[cfg(test)]
   568	mod tests {
   569	    use super::*;
   570	    use crate::proto::canonical_ingest::v1::EventResult;
   571	    use crate::proto::common::v1::Error as ProtoError;
   572	    use std::collections::HashMap;
   573	    use uuid::Uuid;
   574	
   575	    fn fixture_cfg() -> DriftDetectorConfig {
   576	        DriftDetectorConfig {
   577	            drift_z_threshold: 2.0,
   578	            min_samples_for_alert: 100,
   579	        }
   580	    }
   581	
   582	    fn fixture_agg() -> BucketAggregate {
   583	        // R2 M2: baseline_* fields drive z-score. Configure so
   584	        // existing tests' z-values stay numerically identical:
   585	        // current = 150 (mean_7d), baseline = 100, baseline_stddev = 20
   586	        // → z = (150 - 100) / 20 = 2.5
   587	        BucketAggregate {
   588	            tenant_id: Uuid::new_v4(),
   589	            model: "gpt-4o".into(),
   590	            agent_id: "agent-a".into(),
   591	            prompt_class: "chat_short".into(),
   592	            mean_7d: Some(150.0),
   593	            stddev_7d: Some(20.0),
   594	            sample_size_7d: Some(200),
   595	            mean_30d: Some(100.0),
   596	            stddev_30d: Some(20.0),
   597	            sample_size_30d: Some(800),
   598	            baseline_mean: Some(100.0),
   599	            baseline_stddev: Some(20.0),
   600	            baseline_sample_size: Some(600),
   601	        }
   602	    }
   603	
   604	    #[test]
   605	    fn z_score_normal_path() {
   606	        let agg = fixture_agg();
   607	        // (150 - 100) / 20 = 2.5
   608	        let z = compute_z_score(&agg).expect("z_score");
   609	        assert!((z - 2.5).abs() < 1e-4);
   610	    }
   611	
   612	    #[test]
   613	    fn z_score_none_when_missing_baseline_window() {
   614	        // R2 M2: baseline (NOT mean_30d) drives the z calculation.
   615	        let mut agg = fixture_agg();
   616	        agg.baseline_mean = None;
   617	        assert!(compute_z_score(&agg).is_none());
   618	    }
   619	
   620	    #[test]
   621	    fn z_score_none_when_baseline_stddev_zero() {
   622	        // R2 M2: baseline_stddev (NOT stddev_30d) is the denominator.
   623	        let mut agg = fixture_agg();
   624	        agg.baseline_stddev = Some(0.0);
   625	        assert!(compute_z_score(&agg).is_none());
   626	    }
   627	
   628	    #[test]
   629	    fn z_score_none_when_inputs_are_non_finite() {
   630	        let mut agg = fixture_agg();
   631	        agg.mean_7d = Some(f32::NAN);
   632	        assert!(compute_z_score(&agg).is_none());
   633	
   634	        let mut agg = fixture_agg();
   635	        agg.baseline_mean = Some(f32::INFINITY);
   636	        assert!(compute_z_score(&agg).is_none());
   637	
   638	        let mut agg = fixture_agg();
   639	        agg.baseline_stddev = Some(f32::NEG_INFINITY);
   640	        assert!(compute_z_score(&agg).is_none());
   641	    }
   642	
   643	    #[test]
   644	    fn z_score_uses_baseline_excluding_current_window() {
   645	        // R2 M2 regression: mean_30d INCLUDES the current 7d, baseline
   646	        // EXCLUDES it. Verify drift_detector reads from baseline_*
   647	        // (the excluded form), not mean_30d. Construct a case where
   648	        // the two differ + assert the computed z reflects baseline_*.
   649	        let mut agg = fixture_agg();
   650	        agg.mean_30d = Some(140.0); // inclusive mean — close to mean_7d
   651	        agg.stddev_30d = Some(100.0); // inclusive stddev — would damp z
   652	        agg.baseline_mean = Some(100.0); // exclusive baseline
   653	        agg.baseline_stddev = Some(20.0); // exclusive baseline
   654	                                          // z must be (150 - 100) / 20 = 2.5, NOT (150 - 140) / 100 = 0.1.
   655	        let z = compute_z_score(&agg).expect("z");
   656	        assert!((z - 2.5).abs() < 1e-4, "z must use baseline_*, got {z}");
   657	    }
   658	
   659	    #[test]
   660	    fn should_emit_true_above_threshold() {
   661	        let agg = fixture_agg();
   662	        // z = 2.5 > 2.0; n7d = 200 >= 100.
   663	        assert!(should_emit_drift_alert(&agg, &fixture_cfg()));
   664	    }
   665

   667	    fn should_emit_false_below_threshold() {
   668	        let mut agg = fixture_agg();

   670	        agg.mean_7d = Some(110.0); // z = 0.5
   671	        assert!(!should_emit_drift_alert(&agg, &fixture_cfg()));
   672	    }

   678	        assert!(!should_emit_drift_alert(&agg, &fixture_cfg()));
   679	    }

   686	        assert!(!should_emit_drift_alert(&agg, &cfg));
   687	
   688	        cfg.drift_z_threshold = f32::INFINITY;
   689	        assert!(!should_emit_drift_alert(&agg, &cfg));
   690	    }

   696	        agg.mean_7d = Some(50.0); // z = -2.5
   697	        assert!(should_emit_drift_alert(&agg, &fixture_cfg()));
   698	    }

   711	    fn build_drift_alert_uses_audit_routed_prefix() {

   713	        // `spendguard.audit.*` prefix to route into ImmutableAuditLog.

   729	        let parsed: serde_json::Value = serde_json::from_slice(&ce.data).expect("data is json");

   773	    /// In-memory recording sink for the integration-style detect_and_emit
   774	    /// test (no real signer required — uses DisabledSigner).
   775	    struct RecordingSink {
   776	        events: parking_lot::Mutex<Vec<CloudEvent>>,
   777	    }
   778	
   779	    #[async_trait::async_trait]
   780	    impl DriftAlertSink for RecordingSink {
   781	        async fn emit(&self, event: CloudEvent) -> Result<(), anyhow::Error> {
   782	            self.events.lock().push(event);
   783	            Ok(())
   784	        }
   785	    }
   786	
   787	    struct FailingSink;
   788	
   789	    #[async_trait::async_trait]
   790	    impl DriftAlertSink for FailingSink {
   791	        async fn emit(&self, _event: CloudEvent) -> Result<(), anyhow::Error> {
   792	            Err(anyhow::anyhow!("canonical_ingest rejected append"))
   793	        }
   794	    }
   795	
   796	    #[derive(Default)]
   797	    struct MemoryCooldown {
   798	        entries: parking_lot::Mutex<HashMap<DriftAlertKey, DateTime<Utc>>>,
   799	    }
   800	
   801	    #[async_trait::async_trait]
   802	    impl DriftAlertCooldown for MemoryCooldown {
   803	        async fn check(
   804	            &self,
   805	            key: &DriftAlertKey,
   806	            now: DateTime<Utc>,
   807	        ) -> Result<DriftAlertCooldownDecision, anyhow::Error> {
   808	            let entries = self.entries.lock();
   809	            if let Some(existing) = entries.get(key) {
   810	                if *existing > now {
   811	                    return Ok(DriftAlertCooldownDecision::Suppressed {
   812	                        suppress_until: *existing,
   813	                    });
   814	                }
   815	            }
   816	            let suppress_until = now + ChronoDuration::hours(DRIFT_ALERT_COOLDOWN_HOURS);
   817	            Ok(DriftAlertCooldownDecision::Allowed { suppress_until })
   818	        }
   819	
   820	        async fn record_emitted(
   821	            &self,
   822	            key: &DriftAlertKey,
   823	            emitted_at: DateTime<Utc>,
   824	            _z_score: f32,
   825	        ) -> Result<DateTime<Utc>, anyhow::Error> {
   826	            let suppress_until = emitted_at + ChronoDuration::hours(DRIFT_ALERT_COOLDOWN_HOURS);
   827	            let mut entries = self.entries.lock();
   828	            entries.insert(key.clone(), suppress_until);
   829	            Ok(suppress_until)
   830	        }
   831	    }
   832	
   833	    struct FailingCooldown;
   834	
   835	    #[async_trait::async_trait]
   836	    impl DriftAlertCooldown for FailingCooldown {
   837	        async fn check(
   838	            &self,
   839	            _key: &DriftAlertKey,
   840	            _now: DateTime<Utc>,
   841	        ) -> Result<DriftAlertCooldownDecision, anyhow::Error> {
   842	            Err(anyhow::anyhow!("cooldown store unavailable"))
   843	        }
   844	
   845	        async fn record_emitted(
   846	            &self,
   847	            _key: &DriftAlertKey,
   848	            _emitted_at: DateTime<Utc>,
   849	            _z_score: f32,
   850	        ) -> Result<DateTime<Utc>, anyhow::Error> {
   851	            Err(anyhow::anyhow!("cooldown store unavailable"))
   852	        }
   853	    }
   854	
   855	    #[tokio::test]
   856	    async fn memory_cooldown_suppresses_same_key_until_expiry() {
   857	        let store = MemoryCooldown::default();
   858	        let key = DriftAlertKey::from(&fixture_agg());
   859	        let now = Utc::now();
   860	        let first = store.check(&key, now).await.expect("first");
   861	        assert!(matches!(first, DriftAlertCooldownDecision::Allowed { .. }));
   862	        store
   863	            .record_emitted(&key, now, 2.5)
   864	            .await
   865	            .expect("record first");
   866	
   867	        let second = store
   868	            .check(&key, now + ChronoDuration::hours(1))
   869	            .await
   870	            .expect("second");
   871	        assert!(matches!(
   872	            second,
   873	            DriftAlertCooldownDecision::Suppressed { .. }
   874	        ));
   875	
   876	        let after_expiry = store
   877	            .check(&key, now + ChronoDuration::hours(25))
   878	            .await
   879	            .expect("after expiry");
   880	        assert!(matches!(
   881	            after_expiry,
   882	            DriftAlertCooldownDecision::Allowed { .. }
   883	        ));
   884	    }
   885	
   886	    #[tokio::test]
   887	    async fn memory_cooldown_key_is_tenant_model_agent_prompt_scoped() {
   888	        let store = MemoryCooldown::default();
   889	        let agg = fixture_agg();
   890	        let key = DriftAlertKey::from(&agg);
   891	        let now = Utc::now();
   892	        store.record_emitted(&key, now, 2.5).await.expect("seed");
   893	
   894	        let mut different_prompt = key.clone();
   895	        different_prompt.prompt_class = "rag".into();
   896	        assert!(matches!(
   897	            store
   898	                .check(&different_prompt, now + ChronoDuration::hours(1))
   899	                .await
   900	                .expect("different prompt"),
   901	            DriftAlertCooldownDecision::Allowed { .. }
   902	        ));
   903	
   904	        let mut different_tenant = key.clone();
   905	        different_tenant.tenant_id = Uuid::new_v4();
   906	        assert!(matches!(
   907	            store
   908	                .check(&different_tenant, now + ChronoDuration::hours(1))
   909	                .await
   910	                .expect("different tenant"),
   911	            DriftAlertCooldownDecision::Allowed { .. }
   912	        ));
   913	    }
   914	
   915	    fn append_response(status: EventStatus) -> AppendEventsResponse {
   916	        AppendEventsResponse {
   917	            results: vec![EventResult {
   918	                event_id: "evt-1".to_string(),
   919	                status: status as i32,
   920	                ingest_position: None,
   921	                error: Some(ProtoError {
   922	                    code: 0,
   923	                    message: format!("{status:?} for test"),
   924	                    details: Default::default(),
   925	                }),
   926	            }],
   927	        }
   928	    }
   929	
   930	    #[test]
   931	    fn append_response_validation_accepts_durable_statuses() {
   932	        ensure_append_accepted(append_response(EventStatus::Appended)).expect("appended ok");
   933	        ensure_append_accepted(append_response(EventStatus::Deduped)).expect("deduped ok");
   934	    }
   935	
   936	    #[test]
   937	    fn append_response_validation_rejects_quarantined_status() {
   938	        let err = ensure_append_accepted(append_response(EventStatus::Quarantined))
   939	            .expect_err("quarantined is not durable success");
   940	        assert!(
   941	            err.to_string().contains("Quarantined"),
   942	            "error should include status, got {err}"
   943	        );
   944	    }
   945	
   946	    #[test]
   947	    fn append_response_validation_rejects_empty_or_multiple_results() {
   948	        let empty_err = ensure_append_accepted(AppendEventsResponse { results: vec![] })
   949	            .expect_err("missing per-event result must fail");
   950	        assert!(empty_err.to_string().contains("0 results"));
   951	
   952	        let multi_err = ensure_append_accepted(AppendEventsResponse {
   953	            results: vec![
   954	                EventResult {
   955	                    event_id: "evt-1".to_string(),
   956	                    status: EventStatus::Appended as i32,
   957	                    ingest_position: None,
   958	                    error: None,
   959	                },
   960	                EventResult {
   961	                    event_id: "evt-2".to_string(),
   962	                    status: EventStatus::Appended as i32,
   963	                    ingest_position: None,
   964	                    error: None,
   965	                },
   966	            ],
   967	        })
   968	        .expect_err("multiple results for one event must fail");
   969	        assert!(multi_err.to_string().contains("2 results"));
   970	    }

   972	    #[tokio::test]
   973	    async fn detect_and_emit_only_fires_on_breach() {
   974	        use spendguard_signing::DisabledSigner;
   975	        let sink = RecordingSink {
   976	            events: parking_lot::Mutex::new(Vec::new()),
   977	        };
   978	        let cooldown = MemoryCooldown::default();
   979	        let signer = DisabledSigner::for_test("test-stats-aggregator".into());
   980	        let mut breaching = fixture_agg();
   981	        breaching.mean_7d = Some(200.0); // z = 5.0; far above 2.0
   982	        let mut normal = fixture_agg();
   983	        normal.mean_7d = Some(105.0); // z = 0.25; below 2.0
   984	        let aggregates = vec![breaching, normal];
   985	        let outcome = detect_and_emit(&aggregates, &fixture_cfg(), &signer, &sink, &cooldown)
   986	            .await
   987	            .expect("ok");
   988	        assert_eq!(outcome.emitted, 1, "exactly one breach must fire");
   989	        assert_eq!(outcome.suppressed, 0);
   990	        let events = sink.events.lock();
   991	        assert_eq!(events.len(), 1);
   992	        assert_eq!(events[0].r#type, PREDICTION_DRIFT_ALERT_EVENT_TYPE);
   993	    }
   994	
   995	    #[tokio::test]
   996	    async fn detect_and_emit_suppresses_duplicate_same_key() {
   997	        use spendguard_signing::DisabledSigner;
   998	        let sink = RecordingSink {
   999	            events: parking_lot::Mutex::new(Vec::new()),
  1000	        };
  1001	        let cooldown = MemoryCooldown::default();
  1002	        let signer = DisabledSigner::for_test("test-stats-aggregator".into());
  1003	        let mut first = fixture_agg();
  1004	        first.mean_7d = Some(200.0);
  1005	        let second = first.clone();
  1006	
  1007	        let outcome = detect_and_emit(&[first, second], &fixture_cfg(), &signer, &sink, &cooldown)
  1008	            .await
  1009	            .expect("ok");
  1010	
  1011	        assert_eq!(outcome.emitted, 1);
  1012	        assert_eq!(outcome.suppressed, 1);
  1013	        assert_eq!(sink.events.lock().len(), 1);
  1014	    }
  1015	
  1016	    #[tokio::test]
  1017	    async fn detect_and_emit_tenant_isolation_keeps_cooldowns_independent() {
  1018	        use spendguard_signing::DisabledSigner;
  1019	        let sink = RecordingSink {
  1020	            events: parking_lot::Mutex::new(Vec::new()),
  1021	        };
  1022	        let cooldown = MemoryCooldown::default();
  1023	        let signer = DisabledSigner::for_test("test-stats-aggregator".into());
  1024	        let mut tenant_a = fixture_agg();
  1025	        tenant_a.mean_7d = Some(200.0);
  1026	        let mut tenant_b = tenant_a.clone();
  1027	        tenant_b.tenant_id = Uuid::new_v4();
  1028	
  1029	        let outcome = detect_and_emit(
  1030	            &[tenant_a, tenant_b],
  1031	            &fixture_cfg(),
  1032	            &signer,
  1033	            &sink,
  1034	            &cooldown,
  1035	        )
  1036	        .await
  1037	        .expect("ok");
  1038	
  1039	        assert_eq!(outcome.emitted, 2);
  1040	        assert_eq!(outcome.suppressed, 0);
  1041	        assert_eq!(sink.events.lock().len(), 2);
  1042	    }

  1044	    #[tokio::test]
  1045	    async fn detect_and_emit_suppresses_when_cooldown_store_unavailable() {
  1046	        use spendguard_signing::DisabledSigner;
  1047	        let sink = RecordingSink {
  1048	            events: parking_lot::Mutex::new(Vec::new()),
  1049	        };
  1050	        let signer = DisabledSigner::for_test("test-stats-aggregator".into());
  1051	        let mut breaching = fixture_agg();
  1052	        breaching.mean_7d = Some(200.0);
  1053	
  1054	        let outcome = detect_and_emit(
  1055	            &[breaching],
  1056	            &fixture_cfg(),
  1057	            &signer,
  1058	            &sink,
  1059	            &FailingCooldown,
  1060	        )
  1061	        .await
  1062	        .expect("cooldown failure is fail-safe suppression, not cycle failure");
  1063	
  1064	        assert_eq!(outcome.emitted, 0);
  1065	        assert_eq!(outcome.suppressed, 1);
  1066	        assert!(sink.events.lock().is_empty());
  1067	    }
  1068	
  1069	    #[tokio::test]
  1070	    async fn detect_and_emit_does_not_count_failed_append() {

codex
So far the detector’s finite checks cover the values that go into the emitted payload, and the cooldown write is after append success. I’m cross-checking the aggregate source queries and migration role/RLS conventions now, since incompatibility there would only show up in production-like DB behavior.
