/bin/zsh -lc "nl -ba services/stats_aggregator/src/aggregation.rs | sed -n '1,360p'" in /Users/michael.chen/products/agentic-spendguard
 succeeded in 0ms:
     1	//! Aggregation cycle SQL per stats-aggregator-spec-v1alpha1.md §4.1.
     2	//!
     3	//! ## Algorithm
     4	//!
     5	//! Per spec §4.1 the cycle:
     6	//!   1. Acquire Postgres advisory lock (singleton — spec §2.2 + §8.3)
     7	//!   2. For each tenant:
     8	//!        - Run the main 7d+30d aggregation query, UPSERT into
     9	//!          output_distribution_cache
    10	//!        - Run the run-length aggregation query (spec §6), UPSERT into
    11	//!          run_length_distribution_cache
    12	//!        - Run drift detection per bucket (spec §7.1), emit
    13	//!          prediction_drift_alert CloudEvents for buckets with
    14	//!          |z_score| > drift_z_threshold AND sample_size_7d >=
    15	//!          MIN_SAMPLES_FOR_ALERT
    16	//!        - COMMIT per-tenant transaction
    17	//!   3. Release advisory lock
    18	//!
    19	//! ## Per-tenant transaction
    20	//!
    21	//! Spec §8.3 — per-tenant commit ensures one tenant's failure doesn't
    22	//! roll back other tenants' updates. The advisory lock guarantees no
    23	//! other aggregator instance can interleave.
    24	//!
    25	//! ## RLS writer-side discipline (R2 B1)
    26	//!
    27	//! The aggregation writer needs to insert/update rows across all
    28	//! tenants. RLS policy on output_distribution_cache + run_length_*
    29	//! is FOR ALL (R2 B1) — every UPSERT is checked against
    30	//! `app.current_tenant_id`. The writer therefore MUST invoke
    31	//! `SELECT set_config('app.current_tenant_id', '<tenant>', true)`
    32	//! IMMEDIATELY after `pool.begin()` so the per-transaction GUC is
    33	//! visible to subsequent SELECTs + UPSERTs. The R1 shape claimed
    34	//! BYPASSRLS but the role attribute was never granted; FOR ALL +
    35	//! SET LOCAL is the supported path going forward.
    36	
    37	use anyhow::Context;
    38	use sqlx::pool::PoolConnection;
    39	use sqlx::postgres::{PgPool, Postgres};
    40	use sqlx::Row;
    41	use tracing::{debug, info};
    42	use uuid::Uuid;
    43	
    44	/// Identifier for the Postgres advisory lock per spec §8.3. Chosen as a
    45	/// stable random i64 so concurrent stats_aggregator deployments
    46	/// (e.g. blue-green rollout) compete for the same lock; only the lock
    47	/// holder runs the cycle.
    48	pub const STATS_AGGREGATOR_ADVISORY_LOCK_ID: i64 = 0x5350_4441_4747_5253_u64 as i64; // "SPDAGGRS"
    49	
    50	/// One bucket's pre-computed aggregate as read back for drift detection.
    51	///
    52	/// R2 M2 (Software F5): drift_detector::compute_z_score must use a
    53	/// baseline window that EXCLUDES the current 7d window. Spec §7.1:
    54	///
    55	/// ```text
    56	/// baseline_mean = mean over [now - 30d, now - 7d]
    57	/// baseline_stddev = stddev over [now - 30d, now - 7d]
    58	/// current_mean = mean_7d
    59	/// z = (current_mean - baseline_mean) / baseline_stddev
    60	/// ```
    61	///
    62	/// Previously we naively used mean_30d (which INCLUDES the last 7 days)
    63	/// as the baseline — the current window thus contributes to its own
    64	/// reference, biasing z toward 0 and masking real drift. The R2 fix
    65	/// adds dedicated baseline_* fields filled from a separate window query.
    66	#[derive(Debug, Clone)]
    67	pub struct BucketAggregate {
    68	    pub tenant_id: Uuid,
    69	    pub model: String,
    70	    pub agent_id: String,
    71	    pub prompt_class: String,
    72	    pub mean_7d: Option<f32>,
    73	    pub stddev_7d: Option<f32>,
    74	    pub sample_size_7d: Option<i32>,
    75	    pub mean_30d: Option<f32>,
    76	    pub stddev_30d: Option<f32>,
    77	    pub sample_size_30d: Option<i32>,
    78	    /// R2 M2: window over [now - 30d, now - 7d] — baseline used by
    79	    /// drift detector. Distinct from mean_30d (which is the full 30d
    80	    /// window for Strategy B's cache lookup).
    81	    pub baseline_mean: Option<f32>,
    82	    pub baseline_stddev: Option<f32>,
    83	    pub baseline_sample_size: Option<i32>,
    84	}
    85	
    86	/// Acquire the singleton advisory lock per spec §8.3 on a *pinned*
    87	/// PoolConnection.
    88	///
    89	/// R2 B2 (Backend + Software + DB B3): `pg_try_advisory_lock` is
    90	/// session-bound — releasing it on a different connection is a no-op.
    91	/// The R1 shape called `pool.fetch_one()` (arbitrary checkout) for the
    92	/// acquire and `pool.execute()` (different checkout) for the release,
    93	/// so the lock leaked and every subsequent cycle skipped the cycle.
    94	///
    95	/// R2 returns the PoolConnection so the scheduler can hold it for the
    96	/// entire cycle and explicitly release on the same connection. The
    97	/// connection also serves any cycle-wide control queries (per-tenant
    98	/// transactions are still checked out from the pool — `pool.begin()` —
    99	/// because each tenant transaction needs its own commit boundary; the
   100	/// advisory lock itself only needs to live on the pinned connection).
   101	///
   102	/// Returns:
   103	///   * `Ok(Some(conn))` — lock acquired; caller proceeds with the cycle.
   104	///     Caller MUST call [`release_lock_conn`] on the returned connection
   105	///     when done.
   106	///   * `Ok(None)` — another instance holds the lock; caller skips the
   107	///     cycle and emits the stats_aggregator_skipped_lock_held metric.
   108	///
   109	/// Per spec §10 — Postgres session-level locks auto-release on session
   110	/// disconnect, so a panicking scheduler doesn't permanently leak. TCP
   111	/// keepalives govern stale-session detection (~ 2h Linux default;
   112	/// operators tune via tcp_keepalives_idle on the Postgres side).
   113	pub async fn try_acquire_lock_conn(
   114	    pool: &PgPool,
   115	) -> Result<Option<PoolConnection<Postgres>>, anyhow::Error> {
   116	    let mut conn = pool
   117	        .acquire()
   118	        .await
   119	        .context("acquire pool conn for advisory lock")?;
   120	    let acquired: bool = sqlx::query_scalar("SELECT pg_try_advisory_lock($1)")
   121	        .bind(STATS_AGGREGATOR_ADVISORY_LOCK_ID)
   122	        .fetch_one(&mut *conn)
   123	        .await
   124	        .context("pg_try_advisory_lock")?;
   125	    if acquired {
   126	        info!(
   127	            lock_id = STATS_AGGREGATOR_ADVISORY_LOCK_ID,
   128	            "acquired stats_aggregator advisory lock (pinned)"
   129	        );
   130	        Ok(Some(conn))
   131	    } else {
   132	        info!(
   133	            lock_id = STATS_AGGREGATOR_ADVISORY_LOCK_ID,
   134	            "advisory lock held by another instance; skipping cycle"
   135	        );
   136	        // Drop the connection — return it to the pool. Postgres
   137	        // pg_try_advisory_lock returns FALSE without taking the lock,
   138	        // so there is nothing to release.
   139	        drop(conn);
   140	        Ok(None)
   141	    }
   142	}
   143	
   144	/// Release the advisory lock on the connection that acquired it.
   145	///
   146	/// R2 B2: must run on the same session that called
   147	/// `pg_try_advisory_lock` — Postgres bookkeeping is per-session. The
   148	/// connection is then dropped (returned to the pool).
   149	///
   150	/// Failure to release is non-fatal: the lock will release when the
   151	/// session disconnects (immediately on drop here, since we drop the
   152	/// connection at the end). We still surface the error so operators see
   153	/// any anomalies in the cycle metrics.
   154	pub async fn release_lock_conn(mut conn: PoolConnection<Postgres>) -> Result<(), anyhow::Error> {
   155	    sqlx::query("SELECT pg_advisory_unlock($1)")
   156	        .bind(STATS_AGGREGATOR_ADVISORY_LOCK_ID)
   157	        .execute(&mut *conn)
   158	        .await
   159	        .context("pg_advisory_unlock")?;
   160	    debug!(
   161	        lock_id = STATS_AGGREGATOR_ADVISORY_LOCK_ID,
   162	        "released stats_aggregator advisory lock"
   163	    );
   164	    Ok(())
   165	}
   166	
   167	/// Discover the set of distinct tenants present in canonical_events for
   168	/// the last 30 days. Per spec §8.3 we iterate per-tenant for transaction
   169	/// isolation.
   170	///
   171	/// R2 B4: column is `ingest_at` (matches canonical_events.ingest_at —
   172	/// insertion-time stamp) not `recorded_at`. R2 M7: add the
   173	/// recorded_month partition-pruning predicate so the planner stays on
   174	/// the active monthly partitions instead of scanning the default
   175	/// partition.
   176	pub async fn discover_active_tenants(pool: &PgPool) -> Result<Vec<Uuid>, anyhow::Error> {
   177	    let rows = sqlx::query(
   178	        r#"
   179	        SELECT DISTINCT tenant_id
   180	        FROM canonical_events
   181	        WHERE event_type = 'spendguard.audit.outcome'
   182	          AND ingest_at >= now() - interval '30 days'
   183	          AND recorded_month >= DATE_TRUNC('month', now() - interval '30 days')::DATE
   184	        "#,
   185	    )
   186	    .fetch_all(pool)
   187	    .await
   188	    .context("discover_active_tenants")?;
   189	    let tenants = rows
   190	        .into_iter()
   191	        .map(|r| r.get::<Uuid, _>("tenant_id"))
   192	        .collect();
   193	    Ok(tenants)
   194	}
   195	
   196	/// Run the main aggregation cycle for one tenant per spec §4.1. Inserts /
   197	/// updates rows in output_distribution_cache. Returns the list of
   198	/// post-update bucket aggregates so the drift detector can consume them.
   199	pub async fn aggregate_output_distribution(
   200	    pool: &PgPool,
   201	    tenant_id: Uuid,
   202	) -> Result<Vec<BucketAggregate>, anyhow::Error> {
   203	    let mut tx = pool.begin().await.context("begin tenant tx")?;
   204	
   205	    // R2 B1: set RLS session variable per migration 0016 FOR ALL policy.
   206	    // Without this every UPSERT below would fail with "new row violates
   207	    // row-level security policy". `set_config(..., true)` is the
   208	    // SET-LOCAL flavour — the GUC is auto-reset at COMMIT / ROLLBACK.
   209	    sqlx::query("SELECT set_config('app.current_tenant_id', $1, true)")
   210	        .bind(tenant_id.to_string())
   211	        .execute(&mut *tx)
   212	        .await
   213	        .context("set RLS tenant_id for output_distribution_cache")?;
   214	
   215	    // Spec §4.1 main aggregation query. NOTE: we run two queries (7d
   216	    // window + 30d window) and stitch via the application — keeping
   217	    // the SQL simpler than the spec's CTE-with-LEFT-JOIN shape and
   218	    // friendlier to per-tenant transaction commit semantics. The
   219	    // production query plan benchmarks closer to the CTE form because
   220	    // the percentile_cont workload is the dominant cost; the two-phase
   221	    // application stitching adds ~5% overhead vs the full SQL CTE.
   222	    //
   223	    // R2 B3 (Software B4): GROUP BY the 7-class `prompt_class` enum —
   224	    // NOT prompt_class_fingerprint. output-predictor spec §8.2 closing
   225	    // paragraph: "Aggregator key uses class itself, not fingerprint —
   226	    // fingerprint is the audit identifier, class is the aggregation
   227	    // bucket." Stitching by the enum means the predictor's L4 cache
   228	    // lookup (keyed on req.prompt_class) hits real rows.
   229	    //
   230	    // R2 B4 (DB B1): columns referenced are the first-class mirror
   231	    // columns added in 0018 (model, agent_id, prompt_class) — NOT the
   232	    // base64-decoded JSON path. ingest_at not recorded_at (matches the
   233	    // real canonical_events.ingest_at). recorded_month partition-prune
   234	    // predicate (R2 M7) pins the planner to active partitions.
   235	    //
   236	    // GA_07 R5 arbitration: sidecar commit/outcome rows are intentionally
   237	    // sparse; the paired decision row is the authoritative source for
   238	    // predictor bucket mirrors. Aggregate actual output tokens from
   239	    // outcome rows, then join by decision_id to recover the decision's
   240	    // (model, agent_id, prompt_class) when the outcome did not duplicate
   241	    // those fields.
   242	    let agg_30d = sqlx::query(
   243	        r#"
   244	        SELECT
   245	          COALESCE(outcome.model, decision.model) AS model,
   246	          COALESCE(outcome.agent_id, decision.agent_id) AS agent_id,
   247	          COALESCE(outcome.prompt_class, decision.prompt_class) AS prompt_class,
   248	          percentile_cont(0.50) WITHIN GROUP (ORDER BY outcome.actual_output_tokens)::REAL AS p50_30d,
   249	          percentile_cont(0.95) WITHIN GROUP (ORDER BY outcome.actual_output_tokens)::REAL AS p95_30d,
   250	          percentile_cont(0.99) WITHIN GROUP (ORDER BY outcome.actual_output_tokens)::REAL AS p99_30d,
   251	          avg(outcome.actual_output_tokens)::REAL AS mean_30d,
   252	          stddev_samp(outcome.actual_output_tokens)::REAL AS stddev_30d,
   253	          count(*)::INT AS sample_size_30d
   254	        FROM canonical_events outcome
   255	        LEFT JOIN canonical_events decision
   256	          ON decision.tenant_id = outcome.tenant_id
   257	         AND decision.decision_id = outcome.decision_id
   258	         AND decision.event_type = 'spendguard.audit.decision'
   259	         AND decision.recorded_month >= DATE_TRUNC('month', now() - interval '30 days')::DATE
   260	        WHERE outcome.event_type = 'spendguard.audit.outcome'
   261	          AND outcome.actual_output_tokens IS NOT NULL
   262	          AND outcome.ingest_at >= now() - interval '30 days'
   263	          AND outcome.recorded_month >= DATE_TRUNC('month', now() - interval '30 days')::DATE
   264	          AND outcome.tenant_id = $1
   265	          AND COALESCE(outcome.model, decision.model) IS NOT NULL
   266	          AND COALESCE(outcome.agent_id, decision.agent_id) IS NOT NULL
   267	          AND COALESCE(outcome.prompt_class, decision.prompt_class) IS NOT NULL
   268	        GROUP BY 1, 2, 3
   269	        "#,
   270	    )
   271	    .bind(tenant_id)
   272	    .fetch_all(&mut *tx)
   273	    .await
   274	    .context("aggregate 30d window")?;
   275	
   276	    let agg_7d = sqlx::query(
   277	        r#"
   278	        SELECT
   279	          COALESCE(outcome.model, decision.model) AS model,
   280	          COALESCE(outcome.agent_id, decision.agent_id) AS agent_id,
   281	          COALESCE(outcome.prompt_class, decision.prompt_class) AS prompt_class,
   282	          percentile_cont(0.50) WITHIN GROUP (ORDER BY outcome.actual_output_tokens)::REAL AS p50_7d,
   283	          percentile_cont(0.95) WITHIN GROUP (ORDER BY outcome.actual_output_tokens)::REAL AS p95_7d,
   284	          percentile_cont(0.99) WITHIN GROUP (ORDER BY outcome.actual_output_tokens)::REAL AS p99_7d,
   285	          avg(outcome.actual_output_tokens)::REAL AS mean_7d,
   286	          stddev_samp(outcome.actual_output_tokens)::REAL AS stddev_7d,
   287	          count(*)::INT AS sample_size_7d
   288	        FROM canonical_events outcome
   289	        LEFT JOIN canonical_events decision
   290	          ON decision.tenant_id = outcome.tenant_id
   291	         AND decision.decision_id = outcome.decision_id
   292	         AND decision.event_type = 'spendguard.audit.decision'
   293	         AND decision.recorded_month >= DATE_TRUNC('month', now() - interval '7 days')::DATE
   294	        WHERE outcome.event_type = 'spendguard.audit.outcome'
   295	          AND outcome.actual_output_tokens IS NOT NULL
   296	          AND outcome.ingest_at >= now() - interval '7 days'
   297	          AND outcome.recorded_month >= DATE_TRUNC('month', now() - interval '7 days')::DATE
   298	          AND outcome.tenant_id = $1
   299	          AND COALESCE(outcome.model, decision.model) IS NOT NULL
   300	          AND COALESCE(outcome.agent_id, decision.agent_id) IS NOT NULL
   301	          AND COALESCE(outcome.prompt_class, decision.prompt_class) IS NOT NULL
   302	        GROUP BY 1, 2, 3
   303	        "#,
   304	    )
   305	    .bind(tenant_id)
   306	    .fetch_all(&mut *tx)
   307	    .await
   308	    .context("aggregate 7d window")?;
   309	
   310	    // R2 M2: drift baseline — distinct window [now-30d, now-7d] that
   311	    // EXCLUDES the current 7d so the baseline doesn't contaminate the
   312	    // z-score. Same column shape as the other two queries.
   313	    let agg_baseline = sqlx::query(
   314	        r#"
   315	        SELECT
   316	          COALESCE(outcome.model, decision.model) AS model,
   317	          COALESCE(outcome.agent_id, decision.agent_id) AS agent_id,
   318	          COALESCE(outcome.prompt_class, decision.prompt_class) AS prompt_class,
   319	          avg(outcome.actual_output_tokens)::REAL AS baseline_mean,
   320	          stddev_samp(outcome.actual_output_tokens)::REAL AS baseline_stddev,
   321	          count(*)::INT AS baseline_sample_size
   322	        FROM canonical_events outcome
   323	        LEFT JOIN canonical_events decision
   324	          ON decision.tenant_id = outcome.tenant_id
   325	         AND decision.decision_id = outcome.decision_id
   326	         AND decision.event_type = 'spendguard.audit.decision'
   327	         AND decision.recorded_month >= DATE_TRUNC('month', now() - interval '30 days')::DATE
   328	        WHERE outcome.event_type = 'spendguard.audit.outcome'
   329	          AND outcome.actual_output_tokens IS NOT NULL
   330	          AND outcome.ingest_at < now() - interval '7 days'
   331	          AND outcome.ingest_at >= now() - interval '30 days'
   332	          AND outcome.recorded_month >= DATE_TRUNC('month', now() - interval '30 days')::DATE
   333	          AND outcome.tenant_id = $1
   334	          AND COALESCE(outcome.model, decision.model) IS NOT NULL
   335	          AND COALESCE(outcome.agent_id, decision.agent_id) IS NOT NULL
   336	          AND COALESCE(outcome.prompt_class, decision.prompt_class) IS NOT NULL
   337	        GROUP BY 1, 2, 3
   338	        "#,
   339	    )
   340	    .bind(tenant_id)
   341	    .fetch_all(&mut *tx)
   342	    .await
   343	    .context("aggregate drift baseline window [now-30d, now-7d]")?;
   344	
   345	    // Stitch on (model, agent_id, prompt_class).
   346	    use std::collections::HashMap;
   347	    let mut by_key: HashMap<(String, String, String), BucketAggregate> = HashMap::new();
   348	    for r in &agg_30d {
   349	        let k = (
   350	            r.get::<String, _>("model"),
   351	            r.get::<String, _>("agent_id"),
   352	            r.get::<String, _>("prompt_class"),
   353	        );
   354	        by_key.insert(
   355	            k.clone(),
   356	            BucketAggregate {
   357	                tenant_id,
   358	                model: k.0.clone(),
   359	                agent_id: k.1.clone(),
   360	                prompt_class: k.2.clone(),

exec

 # POST_GA 06 - Stats Drift Hygiene
 
 > **Branch**: `post-ga/POST_GA_06_stats_drift_hygiene`
-> **Status**: draft
+> **Status**: implemented; adversarial review pending
 > **Spec ancestor(s)**: `post-ga-backlog-spec-v1alpha1.md`, `stats-aggregator-spec-v1alpha1.md`, `audit-chain-prediction-extension-v1alpha1.md`
 > **Issues**: #157, #162
 > **Estimated change size**: medium; stats aggregation, alert dedup, numeric guards
 
 ---
 
 ## §0. TL;DR
 
-Add prediction drift alert dedup/cooldown and harden drift math against
-NaN/Infinity so the stats aggregator emits stable, bounded alerts.
+Add durable prediction drift alert dedup/cooldown and harden drift math
+against NaN/Infinity so the stats aggregator emits stable, bounded
+alerts.
 
 ## §1. Architectural Context
 
 Stats aggregation converts audit prediction evidence into drift alerts.
 The current GA path works, but post-GA issues call out alert dedup over
 24 hours and numeric safety around invalid floating-point values.
 
 ## §2. Scope
 
 - #157: 24h cooldown/dedup per `(tenant, model, agent_id, prompt_class)`
 - #162: NaN/Infinity guard in drift alert math
 - Tests for dedup, cooldown expiry, tenant isolation, and numeric guards
 - Spec sync for alert source/event behavior only when runtime changes require it
 
 ## §3. Out of Scope
 
 | Item | Pushed to |
 |---|---|
 | Drift event type doc cleanup | POST_GA_02 unless runtime changed |
 | Dashboard/alert rule changes | Future observability slice unless required |
 | Backfill of historical alerts | Not needed for forward hygiene |
 
 ## §4. File-Level Changes
 
 - Modify `services/stats_aggregator/src/**`
 - Add tests under stats aggregator test layout
 - Add SQL migration only if dedup state needs durable storage
 - Update `docs/stats-aggregator-spec-v1alpha1.md` if behavior changes
 - Add evidence under `docs/reviews/post-ga/POST_GA_06_stats_drift_hygiene/`
 
 ## §5. Schema / Proto
 
 Prefer an existing table or deterministic idempotency key if available.
-If durable cooldown state is required, add a forward migration with a
-unique key on the dedup dimensions and indexed expiry/last_emitted time.
-No proto changes expected.
+Durable cooldown state lands in
+`services/canonical_ingest/migrations/0022_prediction_drift_alert_cooldowns.sql`.
+The primary key is exactly `(tenant_id, model, agent_id, prompt_class)`;
+`suppress_until` is indexed for expiry inspection. Key constraints mirror
+the `canonical_events` aggregator mirror columns, including
+character-count limits for multibyte-safe `agent_id` values and the same
+7-class `prompt_class` enum. RLS is enabled and forced with a `FOR ALL`
+policy using `app.current_tenant_id`; missing or invalid tenant context
+fails closed. `last_z_score` rejects `NaN` and `+/-Infinity` at both
+runtime and SQL CHECK layers. No proto changes.
 
 ## §6. Audit-Chain Impact
 
-Dedup suppresses duplicate alert emission; it must not delete or mutate
-prior audit rows. Suppressed decisions should be observable through
-metrics or logs so operators can distinguish quiet cooldown from no
-drift.
+Dedup suppresses duplicate alert emission; it does not delete or mutate
+prior audit rows. Suppressed decisions are observable through structured
+logs and `spendguard_stats_aggregator_drift_alerts_suppressed_total`, so
+operators can distinguish quiet cooldown from no drift.
 
 ## §7. Failure Modes
 
 | Scenario | Expected behavior |
 |---|---|
 | Same drift repeats within 24h | Suppress duplicate alert |
 | Same drift after cooldown | Emit once |
 | Different tenant/model/agent/prompt | Independent cooldown key |
-| Drift math produces NaN/Infinity | Skip or clamp according to documented fail-closed rule |
-| Dedup store unavailable | Fail safe; do not spam immutable audit |
+| Drift math produces NaN/Infinity | Fail closed; no audit payload or cooldown row is written |
+| Dedup store unavailable before emit | Fail safe; do not spam immutable audit |
+| Immutable append fails | No cooldown row is recorded; retry next cycle |
+| Cooldown record fails after append | Alert remains durable; log duplicate-suppression risk |
 
 ## §8. Acceptance Gates
 
 - `cargo build && cargo test` for `services/stats_aggregator`
 - Dedup/cooldown tests cover same-key and different-key behavior
 - Numeric tests cover NaN, Infinity, and division-by-zero cases
 - Migration smoke if SQL added
 - `make demo-up DEMO_MODE=default` if aggregator runtime path changes
 - Evidence under `docs/reviews/post-ga/POST_GA_06_stats_drift_hygiene/`
 
+Executed evidence is recorded in
+`docs/reviews/post-ga/POST_GA_06_stats_drift_hygiene/verification.md`.
+
 ## §9. Review Checklist
 
 1. Is the dedup key exactly `(tenant, model, agent_id, prompt_class)`?
 2. Is the cooldown 24h and testable without sleeping?
 3. Are NaN and Infinity impossible to emit into audit payloads?
 4. Is tenant isolation preserved?
 5. Does failure mode avoid alert spam?
 
 ## §10. Deferrals
 
 | Item | Why deferred |
 |---|---|
 | Historical backfill | Not needed for forward alert hygiene |
 | UI surfacing of suppressed alerts | Product UX |
 
 ## §11. Risk / Rollback
 
 Risk is suppressing real alerts or spamming audit. Keep dedup key and
 cooldown explicit, with metrics for suppression. Roll back by disabling
 dedup only if alert spam risk is controlled by another gate.
 
 ## §12. AIT Execution Notes
 
-Reviewer: codex CLI via `ait run --adapter codex --review-mode adversarial`. Max 5 rounds. Staff+ panel arbitration if 5 rounds fail.
+Reviewer: codex CLI via `ait run --adapter codex --review adversarial`. Max 5 rounds. Staff+ panel arbitration if 5 rounds fail.
 
 Reviewer should inspect dedup persistence and numeric edge tests.
 
 ## §13. Adoption History
 
 | Role | Decision | Outcome |
 |---|---|---|
 | Software Architect | Keep stats hygiene separate from doc-only drift sync | POST_GA_06 vs POST_GA_02 |
 | Backend Architect | Cooldown key must match issue text exactly | §7 |
 | Security Engineer | Alert suppression must not hide tenant isolation issues | §9 |
 | Database Optimizer | Durable dedup needs a bounded indexed key | §5 |
 | Stats Domain Expert | NaN/Infinity are invalid alert payload values | §7 |
+| Implementer self-review | PostgreSQL treats `NaN = NaN` as true, so the SQL CHECK must explicitly compare against `'NaN'::REAL` | Migration 0022 + `drift_alert_cooldown_postgres_rejects_non_finite_z_scores` |
+| Staff+ panel | Store-unavailable behavior favors suppressing duplicates over immutable audit spam | §6/§7 |

+++ b/docs/stats-aggregator-spec-v1alpha1.md
@@ -314,186 +314,228 @@ CREATE POLICY output_distribution_cache_tenant_isolation
 ```
 
 `sample_size_7d` and `sample_size_30d` are COUNT-derived values and must
 never be negative. Migrations pin this with CHECK constraints; the
 aggregation writer only produces non-negative integers, and migration
 smoke checks assert explicit negative inserts are rejected.
 
 ### 5.1 為什麼不 partition
 
 - 量級 estimate：1000 tenants × 20 models × 50 agents × 7 classes = 7M rows max（per-tenant 7K rows）
 - ON CONFLICT UPSERT 在 7M 表現良好
 - Partition 帶來複雜度（partition pruning + cross-partition uniqueness）不值得
 
 若未來 multi-tenant 規模超 50K tenants，再 partition by `(tenant_id % 256)` 或類似 hash partitioning。
 
 ---
 
 ## §6. (tenant, agent_id) run-length distribution
 
 per `run-cost-projector-spec-v1alpha1.md` Signal 1 需要 「per-(tenant, agent_id) historical P95 run-length（steps per run）」。stats_aggregator 同 cycle 算這個 distribution：
 
 ```sql
 WITH run_lengths AS (
   SELECT
     tenant_id,
     agent_id,
     run_id_mirror AS run_id,
     count(*) AS steps_in_run
   FROM canonical_events
   WHERE event_type = 'spendguard.audit.decision'
     AND ingest_at >= now() - interval '30 days'
     AND recorded_month >= DATE_TRUNC('month', now() - interval '30 days')::DATE
     AND agent_id IS NOT NULL
     AND run_id_mirror IS NOT NULL
   GROUP BY tenant_id, agent_id, run_id
 )
 INSERT INTO run_length_distribution_cache (
   tenant_id, agent_id,
   p50_steps_30d, p95_steps_30d, p99_steps_30d,
   mean_steps_30d, stddev_steps_30d, sample_size_30d,
   computed_at, aggregation_version
 )
 SELECT
   tenant_id, agent_id,
   percentile_cont(0.50) WITHIN GROUP (ORDER BY steps_in_run),
   percentile_cont(0.95) WITHIN GROUP (ORDER BY steps_in_run),
   percentile_cont(0.99) WITHIN GROUP (ORDER BY steps_in_run),
   avg(steps_in_run)::REAL,
   stddev_samp(steps_in_run)::REAL,
   count(*),
   now(), 'v1alpha1'
 FROM run_lengths
 GROUP BY tenant_id, agent_id
 ON CONFLICT (tenant_id, agent_id)
   DO UPDATE SET
     p50_steps_30d = EXCLUDED.p50_steps_30d,
     p95_steps_30d = EXCLUDED.p95_steps_30d,
     -- ...
     computed_at = EXCLUDED.computed_at;
 ```
 
 Table schema 與 `output_distribution_cache` 平行（同 RLS + 同新鮮度 index）。`sample_size_30d`
 is also constrained as `CHECK (sample_size_30d IS NULL OR sample_size_30d >= 0)`;
 it is derived from `count(*)` and is therefore a non-negative hard
 invariant, not an advisory convention.
 
 ---
 
 ## §7. Drift detection
 
 ### 7.1 演算法
 
 ```
 For each (tenant, model, agent_id, prompt_class) bucket:
   baseline_mean = mean_30d (excluding last 7 days; computed via separate query window)
   baseline_stddev = stddev_30d (same exclusion)
   current_mean = mean_7d
 
   z_score = (current_mean - baseline_mean) / baseline_stddev
 
-  IF |z_score| > 2.0 AND sample_size_7d >= MIN_SAMPLES_FOR_ALERT:
+  IF z_score is finite
+     AND |z_score| > 2.0
+     AND sample_size_7d >= MIN_SAMPLES_FOR_ALERT
+     AND cooldown key is not active:
     emit prediction_drift_alert
 ```
 
 `MIN_SAMPLES_FOR_ALERT` default 100。理由：小樣本 7-day window 容易 false-positive。
 
+POST_GA_06 runtime hardening: `mean_7d`, `baseline_mean`,
+`baseline_stddev`, `drift_z_threshold`, and the computed `z_score` must
+all be finite. `NaN`, `+Infinity`, `-Infinity`, zero/negative baseline
+stddev, and non-positive/non-finite thresholds fail closed by suppressing
+alert emission; these values must never appear in an immutable
+`prediction_drift_alert` payload.
+
+### 7.1.1 Alert cooldown / dedup
+
+`prediction_drift_alert` emission is rate-limited by a rolling 24-hour
+cooldown keyed exactly on:
+
+```text
+(tenant_id, model, agent_id, prompt_class)
+```
+
+The durable state table is
+`prediction_drift_alert_cooldowns` in the canonical_ingest DB. It is
+mutable derived state, not audit history. The stats_aggregator checks the
+cooldown before attempting alert emission and records the new cooldown
+only after the immutable alert append succeeds; if the same key breaches
+again while `suppress_until > now()`, the alert is suppressed and the
+suppression is observable through logs and
+`spendguard_stats_aggregator_drift_alerts_suppressed_total`.
+
+Failure mode: if the cooldown store is unavailable, stats_aggregator
+suppresses the alert rather than emitting duplicate immutable audit rows.
+This favors avoiding alert spam over retrying a possibly repeated alert.
+If the immutable append fails, no cooldown row is recorded and the next
+cycle can retry the alert. If append succeeds but cooldown recording
+fails, the alert remains durable and the daemon logs that future duplicate
+suppression may be unavailable. Different tenants, models, agents, and
+prompt classes have independent cooldowns.
+
 ### 7.2 Alert event schema
 
 ```yaml
 # CloudEvent emitted to canonical_ingest
 type: spendguard.audit.prediction_drift_alert.v1alpha1
 source: spendguard://stats-aggregator/<tenant_id>
 data:
   tenant_id: <uuid>
   model: <string>
   agent_id: <string>
   prompt_class: <string>
   baseline_period: { start: <ts>, end: <ts>, mean: <float>, stddev: <float> }
   current_period: { start: <ts>, end: <ts>, mean: <float> }
   z_score: <float>
   sample_size_7d: <int>
   sample_size_30d: <int>
   suggested_action: "review_predictor_baseline" | "retrain_strategy_c_plugin" | "investigate_agent_change"
 ```
 
-Implementation reference: `services/stats_aggregator/src/drift_detector.rs` commit `f8dc34c` defines `PREDICTION_DRIFT_ALERT_EVENT_TYPE = "spendguard.audit.prediction_drift_alert.v1alpha1"`; calibration-report commit `8ee35ca` reads the same event type from `canonical_events.payload_json`.
+Implementation reference: `services/stats_aggregator/src/drift_detector.rs`
+commit `f8dc34c` defines `PREDICTION_DRIFT_ALERT_EVENT_TYPE =
+"spendguard.audit.prediction_drift_alert.v1alpha1"`; POST_GA_06 merge
+updates the same module with 24h cooldown and non-finite guard behavior.
+Calibration-report commit `8ee35ca` reads the same event type from
+`canonical_events.payload_json`.
 
 Audit routing discipline: the `spendguard.audit.*` prefix is required so
 canonical_ingest routes this event to ImmutableAuditLog. The source URI
 format matches `build_drift_alert`: `spendguard://stats-aggregator/<tenant_id>`.
 Older examples that used `stats-aggregator://<instance>` or
 `spendguard.prediction.drift_alert` are non-authoritative draft text.
 
 Signed + immutable per audit chain。
 
 ### 7.3 Suggested action 規則
 
 | 條件 | suggested_action |
 |---|---|
 | z_score > 0（actual 高於 baseline）且 baseline 多月穩定 | `investigate_agent_change` |
 | z_score < 0（actual 低於 baseline）且 Tier 1 shadow 也有 drift | `review_predictor_baseline`（vendor tokenizer 升級可能性） |
 | z_score > 0 且 customer 有 Strategy C plugin | `retrain_strategy_c_plugin` |
 
 純 heuristic；operator 仍要人工判斷。
 
 ### 7.4 與 `RUN_DRIFT_DETECTED` 區別
 
 per `contract-dsl-spec-v1alpha2.md` §3.2 明確分離：
 
 - `prediction_drift_alert`（本 spec）：**bucket-level**，hourly aggregation 後 emit；不阻擋 decisions
 - `RUN_DRIFT_DETECTED`（contract-dsl v1alpha2）：**run-instance-level**，hot-path emit；可阻擋 decisions
 
 兩者各有 audit row；calibration-report 同時聚合。
 
 ---
 
 ## §8. Scheduling
 
 ### 8.1 Default
 
 每 1 hour 跑一次完整 aggregation cycle。對所有 tenants × all buckets 一次。
 
 ### 8.2 Per-tenant override
 
 控制 plane API `POST /tenants/{id}/stats-aggregator-cadence { cadence_minutes: 30 }` 允許 tenant 拉高頻次（最低 15 min；保護 DB）。
 
 每次 cycle 對該 tenant 多跑 1 次（按 tenant_id 過濾）；其他 tenants 仍走 1h cadence。
 
 ### 8.3 Aggregation cycle structure
 
 ```
 cycle_start:
   ACQUIRE_ADVISORY_LOCK('stats_aggregator_singleton')  -- only one instance runs
   FOR EACH tenant_id:
     BEGIN
       run main aggregation query (per §4.1)
       run run-length aggregation query (per §6)
       run drift detection per bucket (per §7.1)
       emit drift_alert events as needed
       RECORD cycle metadata (computed_at, sample_sizes, alerts_emitted)
     COMMIT (per tenant; isolation)
   RELEASE_ADVISORY_LOCK
 cycle_end
 ```
 
 Per-tenant commit 確保部分 tenant 失敗不影響其他。
 
 ---
 
 ## §9. Multi-tenant isolation
 
 ### 9.1 機制
 
 1. **Row-Level Security** on cache tables（per §5）
 2. **Connection-level `app.current_tenant_id` setting** before any cache query
 3. **No cross-tenant SQL**：每個 query 必含 explicit `tenant_id = $tenant_var`
 4. **Aggregation cycle isolation**：每 tenant 自己 transaction；不 batch cross-tenant aggregation
 
 ### 9.2 Adversarial test
 
 SLICE 06 acceptance 必含「cross-tenant injection attempt」test：
 
 ```sql
 -- Attempt to read tenant A's cache as tenant B
 SET app.current_tenant_id = '<tenant_b_uuid>';
 SELECT * FROM output_distribution_cache WHERE tenant_id = '<tenant_a_uuid>';

exec

    40	CREATE TABLE run_length_distribution_cache (
    41	    -- Bucket key (per spec §6). (tenant_id, agent_id) tuple — no model
    42	    -- or prompt_class because run length is per-agent semantic property
    43	    -- not per-call semantic property.

    59	    computed_at          TIMESTAMPTZ NOT NULL,

    76	    ON run_length_distribution_cache
    77	    FOR ALL

    95	-- R2 M16: REVOKE SELECT alongside INSERT/UPDATE/DELETE so PUBLIC role
    96	-- doesn't inherit cross-tenant read capability behind RLS. Defence in depth.
    97	REVOKE SELECT, INSERT, UPDATE, DELETE ON run_length_distribution_cache FROM PUBLIC;
    98	
    99	GRANT SELECT, INSERT, UPDATE, DELETE

    70	CREATE TABLE output_distribution_cache (
    71	    -- Bucket key (per spec §3.2 + §5). Tenant UUID per the rest of the
    72	    -- ledger schema convention (0003 budget_window_instances etc.). Model
    73	    -- + agent_id + prompt_class are opaque application-defined identifiers;
    74	    -- TEXT to remain bytes-clean for non-Latin model strings.
    75	    tenant_id            UUID NOT NULL,
    76	    model                TEXT NOT NULL,
    77	    agent_id             TEXT NOT NULL,
    78	    prompt_class         TEXT NOT NULL,
    79	
    80	    -- 7-day rolling window (per spec §4.2 drift detection signal)
    81	    p50_7d               REAL,
    82	    p95_7d               REAL,
    83	    p99_7d               REAL,
    84	    mean_7d              REAL,
    85	    stddev_7d            REAL,
    86	    sample_size_7d       INTEGER CHECK (sample_size_7d IS NULL OR sample_size_7d >= 0),
    87	
    88	    -- 30-day rolling window (per spec §4.2 Strategy B baseline)
    89	    p50_30d              REAL,
    90	    p95_30d              REAL,
    91	    p99_30d              REAL,
    92	    mean_30d             REAL,
    93	    stddev_30d           REAL,
    94	    sample_size_30d      INTEGER CHECK (sample_size_30d IS NULL OR sample_size_30d >= 0),
    95	
    96	    -- Metadata. computed_at TZ-explicit +00 per SLICE_01 R5 convention.
    97	    -- aggregation_version lets downstream consumers detect SQL aggregation
    98	    -- recipe changes (spec §0.1 compatibility policy "aggregation_version
    99	    -- column versioned").
   100	    --
   101	    -- R2 M17: no DEFAULT on computed_at — the aggregation writer ALWAYS
   102	    -- supplies an explicit `now()`. Removing the default closes a foot-gun
   103	    -- where an ad-hoc INSERT could land with `clock_timestamp()` from
   104	    -- within a long-running transaction (the freshness gate downstream
   105	    -- relies on the writer-supplied stamp being the real cycle time).
   106	    computed_at          TIMESTAMPTZ NOT NULL,
   107	    aggregation_version  TEXT NOT NULL DEFAULT 'v1alpha1',
   108	
   109	    PRIMARY KEY (tenant_id, model, agent_id, prompt_class)
   110	);
   111	
   112	-- Freshness lookup: "find buckets where computed_at < cutoff" supports
   113	-- both the SLO freshness alert and the rebuild trigger.
   114	CREATE INDEX output_distribution_cache_freshness_idx
   115	    ON output_distribution_cache (computed_at);
   116	
   117	-- Tenant lookup index — Strategy B hot-path query (per spec §4.2).
   118	-- Covers the most-common WHERE shape (tenant_id, model, agent_id,
   119	-- prompt_class). PRIMARY KEY already covers (tenant_id, model,
   120	-- agent_id, prompt_class) so this index is redundant for equality;
   121	-- kept commented to document the access pattern — the optimiser
   122	-- uses the PK btree directly.
   123	-- CREATE INDEX output_distribution_cache_tenant_lookup_idx ...
   124	
   125	-- ============================================================================
   126	-- Row-Level Security (spec §9.1 mechanism 1).
   127	--
   128	-- Policy: `tenant_id = current_setting('app.current_tenant_id')::uuid`
   129	-- means the calling session MUST have run
   130	-- `SET LOCAL app.current_tenant_id = '<uuid>'` inside its transaction
   131	-- before the SELECT. output_predictor::cache.rs does this per Predict
   132	-- call. Operators querying ad-hoc via psql see 0 rows until they set
   133	-- the session variable explicitly.
   134	--
   135	-- R2 B1 fix: writer uses `set_config('app.current_tenant_id', ...)` per
   136	-- tenant before UPSERT — the policy's WITH CHECK clause then enforces
   137	-- tenant_id matches the session variable at INSERT/UPDATE. No BYPASSRLS
   138	-- attribute on the role; defense-in-depth via FORCE RLS. Adversarial
   139	-- cross-tenant query injection (per spec §9.2) is blocked by the policy
   140	-- + session variable contract for both reads and writes.
   141	-- ============================================================================
   142	
   143	ALTER TABLE output_distribution_cache ENABLE ROW LEVEL SECURITY;
   144	ALTER TABLE output_distribution_cache FORCE ROW LEVEL SECURITY;
   145	
   146	-- R2 B1: FOR ALL policy. SELECT path enforces USING; INSERT/UPDATE/DELETE
   147	-- additionally enforces WITH CHECK so a writer who forgets the SET LOCAL
   148	-- still fails closed (cannot insert a row with mismatched tenant_id).
   149	--
   150	-- Use COALESCE to a deliberately-illegal sentinel so a missing session
   151	-- variable produces a clean tenant-mismatch (returns 0 rows / rejects
   152	-- WITH CHECK) rather than silently leaking every tenant's rows under a
   153	-- NULL match. The sentinel '00000000-0000-0000-0000-000000000000' is
   154	-- the nil UUID which never matches any production tenant_id (all
   155	-- tenants mint UUIDv7 with timestamp > 0).
   156	CREATE POLICY output_distribution_cache_tenant_isolation
   157	    ON output_distribution_cache
   158	    FOR ALL
   159	    USING (
   160	        tenant_id = COALESCE(
   161	            NULLIF(current_setting('app.current_tenant_id', TRUE), ''),
   162	            '00000000-0000-0000-0000-000000000000'
   163	        )::uuid
   164	    )
   165	    WITH CHECK (
   166	        tenant_id = COALESCE(
   167	            NULLIF(current_setting('app.current_tenant_id', TRUE), ''),
   168	            '00000000-0000-0000-0000-000000000000'
   169	        )::uuid
   170	    );
   171	
   172	-- ============================================================================
   173	-- Privilege boundary (mirror of 0005_immutability_triggers.sql convention).
   174	--
   175	-- canonical_ingest_application_role: stats_aggregator (writer) +
   176	--   output_predictor pool (reader path goes through application role to
   177	--   pick up RLS — reader role bypasses RLS by default which is wrong here).
   178	-- canonical_ingest_reader_role: ad-hoc operator queries. RLS applies.
   179	--
   180	-- R2 M16: REVOKE SELECT FROM PUBLIC. Without it, any new connecting role
   181	-- inherits PUBLIC's SELECT and can run cross-tenant probes through the
   182	-- RLS policy. Belt-and-suspenders on top of RLS — defence in depth.
   183	-- ============================================================================
   184	
   185	REVOKE SELECT, INSERT, UPDATE, DELETE ON output_distribution_cache FROM PUBLIC;
   186	
   187	GRANT SELECT, INSERT, UPDATE, DELETE
   188	    ON output_distribution_cache
   189	    TO canonical_ingest_application_role;
   190	
   191	GRANT SELECT ON output_distribution_cache TO canonical_ingest_reader_role;
   192	
   193	COMMENT ON TABLE output_distribution_cache IS
   194	    'Strategy B per-(tenant, model, agent_id, prompt_class) P50/P95/P99 distribution cache per stats-aggregator-spec-v1alpha1.md §5. Populated hourly by services/stats_aggregator. Read by services/output_predictor on the Predict hot path. RLS FOR ALL policy enforces per-tenant isolation at both read AND write time; the stats_aggregator writer SETs app.current_tenant_id per tenant before each UPSERT (R2 B1). Mutable (UPSERT every cycle); no immutability trigger.';
   195	COMMENT ON COLUMN output_distribution_cache.tenant_id IS
   196	    'Tenant identifier (UUIDv7). Indexed via PRIMARY KEY for hot-path Predict lookup.';
   197	COMMENT ON COLUMN output_distribution_cache.computed_at IS
   198	    'Last successful aggregation cycle timestamp. Stale > 2h treated as cache miss by output_predictor per output-predictor-service-spec-v1alpha1.md §4.2.';
   199	COMMENT ON COLUMN output_distribution_cache.aggregation_version IS
   200	    'Aggregation recipe version; rows produced by older recipes can be invalidated by output_predictor when the spec version bumps.';
   201	
   202	-- ============================================================================
   203	-- DO-block smoke check: verify RLS is actually enabled and the policy
   204	-- exists. CVE-2018-1058 hardening: SET LOCAL search_path so PostgreSQL
   205	-- resolves built-in catalog names (pg_catalog.pg_class etc.) without
   206	-- consulting a search_path that an adversary might have injected
   207	-- (per SLICE_01 R5).
   208	-- ============================================================================
   209	DO $$
   210	BEGIN

   694	        sqlx::query("SELECT set_config('app.current_tenant_id', $1, true)")

   698	            .expect("set tenant");

   700	            r#"

   702	              tenant_id, model, agent_id, prompt_class,

   704	            )

   706	              $1, 'gpt-4o-mini', 'agent-alpha', $2,
   707	              clock_timestamp(), clock_timestamp() + interval '24 hours', {value}
   708	            )
   709	            "#
   710	        );
   711	        let err = sqlx::query(&query)
   712	            .bind(tenant_id)
   713	            .bind("chat_short")
   714	            .execute(&mut *tx)
   715	            .await
   716	            .expect_err("non-finite z-score must violate CHECK");
   717	        assert!(
   718	            err.to_string()
   719	                .contains("prediction_drift_alert_cooldowns_last_z_score_check"),

   721	        );
   722	        let _ = tx.rollback().await;
   723	    }

    69	    // R1 shape sent `..Default::default()` on these → empty producer_id

    99	impl std::fmt::Debug for Config {
   100	    /// Mask database_url + sink TLS material paths in Debug output to
   101	    /// avoid leaking secrets into structured logs / panic backtraces.
   102	    fn fmt(&self, f: &mut std::fmt::Formatter<'_>) -> std::fmt::Result {
   103	        f.debug_struct("Config")
   104	            .field("database_url_present", &!self.database_url.is_empty())
   105	            .field("canonical_ingest_url", &self.canonical_ingest_url)
   106	            .field("cycle_seconds", &self.cycle_seconds)
   107	            .field("min_samples_for_alert", &self.min_samples_for_alert)
   108	            .field("drift_z_threshold", &self.drift_z_threshold)
   109	            .field("metrics_addr", &self.metrics_addr)
   110	            .field("region", &self.region)
   111	            .field("profile", &self.profile)
   112	            .field("sink_tls_cert_pem", &self.sink_tls_cert_pem)
   113	            .field("sink_tls_key_pem", &self.sink_tls_key_pem)
   114	            .field("sink_tls_ca_pem", &self.sink_tls_ca_pem)
   115	            .field("sink_tls_sni", &self.sink_tls_sni)
   116	            .field("event_source_override", &self.event_source_override)
   117	            .field("schema_bundle_id", &self.schema_bundle_id)
   118	            .field(
   119	                "schema_bundle_hash_hex_present",
   120	                &!self.schema_bundle_hash_hex.is_empty(),
   121	            )
   122	            .field("canonical_schema_version", &self.canonical_schema_version)
   123	            .finish()
   124	    }
   125	}

    60	-- ============================================================================
    61	
    62	-- Step 1: Add five NULLable mirror columns. TEXT for model / agent_id
    63	-- / prompt_class / prompt_class_fingerprint because the upstream
    64	-- producers carry them as TEXT in CloudEvent JSON. UUID for run_id
    65	-- because it's structurally a UUID per spec §4.1.
    66	ALTER TABLE canonical_events
    67	    ADD COLUMN model                     TEXT,
    68	    ADD COLUMN agent_id                  TEXT,
    69	    ADD COLUMN run_id_mirror             UUID,
    70	    ADD COLUMN prompt_class              TEXT,
    71	    ADD COLUMN prompt_class_fingerprint  TEXT;
    72	
    73	-- Step 2: Length + enum validation. Same shape as 0013's prediction
    74	-- column CHECKs — NOT VALID (avoid re-scan of existing data) + then
    75	-- VALIDATE so new rows are still rejected at write time.
    76	
    77	ALTER TABLE canonical_events
    78	    ADD CONSTRAINT canonical_events_model_length_chk
    79	        CHECK (model IS NULL OR (char_length(model) <= 64 AND char_length(model) > 0))
    80	        NOT VALID,
    81	    ADD CONSTRAINT canonical_events_agent_id_length_chk
    82	        CHECK (agent_id IS NULL OR (char_length(agent_id) <= 128 AND char_length(agent_id) > 0))
    83	        NOT VALID,
    84	    ADD CONSTRAINT canonical_events_prompt_class_enum_chk
    85	        CHECK (prompt_class IS NULL OR prompt_class IN (
    86	            'chat_short', 'chat_long', 'code_gen', 'summarization',
    87	            'rag', 'tool_calling', 'vision'))
    88	        NOT VALID,
    89	    ADD CONSTRAINT canonical_events_prompt_class_fingerprint_length_chk
    90	        CHECK (prompt_class_fingerprint IS NULL
    91	               OR (char_length(prompt_class_fingerprint) BETWEEN 4 AND 256))
    92	        NOT VALID;
    93	
    94	ALTER TABLE canonical_events VALIDATE CONSTRAINT canonical_events_model_length_chk;
    95	ALTER TABLE canonical_events VALIDATE CONSTRAINT canonical_events_agent_id_length_chk;
    96	ALTER TABLE canonical_events VALIDATE CONSTRAINT canonical_events_prompt_class_enum_chk;
    97	ALTER TABLE canonical_events VALIDATE CONSTRAINT canonical_events_prompt_class_fingerprint_length_chk;
    98	
    99	-- Step 3: Partition-prunable composite index for the stats_aggregator
   100	-- hot aggregation query shape. Per-bucket UPSERT cycle filters by
   101	-- (tenant_id, event_type='spendguard.audit.outcome', ingest_at window).
   102	-- Group by (model, agent_id, prompt_class). Partition pruning via
   103	-- recorded_month lifts the active partition set.
   104	CREATE INDEX canonical_events_aggregator_bucket_idx
   105	    ON canonical_events (tenant_id, recorded_month, model, agent_id, prompt_class)

 succeeded in 0ms:
    /// * `signing_key_id` — the AppendEventsRequest batch-level
    ///   signing_key_id (transport check only; per-event signature is on
    ///   each CloudEvent). Reuse signer.key_id() for consistency.
    pub fn new(
        channel: Channel,
        producer_id: String,
        schema_bundle_ref: SchemaBundleRef,
        signing_key_id: String,
    ) -> Self {
        Self {
            client: tokio::sync::Mutex::new(CanonicalIngestClient::new(channel)),
            producer_id,
            schema_bundle_ref,
            signing_key_id,
        }
    }
}

#[async_trait::async_trait]
impl DriftAlertSink for CanonicalIngestDriftAlertSink {
    async fn emit(&self, event: CloudEvent) -> Result<(), anyhow::Error> {
        let req = AppendEventsRequest {
            producer_id: self.producer_id.clone(),
            batch_max_producer_sequence: 0,
            // batch_signature optional per Trace §13 — per-event
            // signature on each CloudEvent is the canonical truth; we
            // leave batch_signature empty (allowed in-cluster on mTLS).
            batch_signature: bytes::Bytes::new(),
            signing_key_id: self.signing_key_id.clone(),
            schema_bundle: Some(self.schema_bundle_ref.clone()),
            events: vec![event],
            // OBSERVABILITY: drift_alert is an observability event, not
            // an enforcement event. canonical_ingest's failure mode for
            // OBSERVABILITY is buffer_then_retry (per Trace §10.1).
            route: Route::Observability as i32,
        };
        let mut guard = self.client.lock().await;
        let resp = guard
            .append_events(req)
            .await
            .context("canonical_ingest AppendEvents")?;
        ensure_append_accepted(resp.into_inner())
    }
}

pub(crate) fn ensure_append_accepted(resp: AppendEventsResponse) -> Result<(), anyhow::Error> {
    if resp.results.len() != 1 {
        return Err(anyhow::anyhow!(
            "AppendEvents prediction_drift_alert returned {} results for one event",
            resp.results.len()
        ));
    }

    let result = &resp.results[0];
    let status = EventStatus::try_from(result.status).unwrap_or(EventStatus::Unspecified);
    match status {
        EventStatus::Appended | EventStatus::Deduped => Ok(()),
        other => {
            let error_message = result
                .error
                .as_ref()
                .map(|err| err.message.as_str())
                .filter(|msg| !msg.is_empty())
                .unwrap_or("canonical_ingest returned no error detail");
            warn!(
                event_id = %result.event_id,
                status = ?other,
                err = %error_message,
                "canonical_ingest rejected prediction_drift_alert"
            );
            Err(anyhow::anyhow!(
                "AppendEvents prediction_drift_alert rejected event_id={} status={:?}: {}",
                result.event_id,
                other,
                error_message
            ))
        }
    }
}

/// Detect drift across a batch of bucket aggregates and emit signed
/// CloudEvents for those exceeding the threshold. Returns the count of
/// alerts emitted for metrics.
pub async fn detect_and_emit(
    aggregates: &[BucketAggregate],
    cfg: &DriftDetectorConfig,
    signer: &dyn Signer,
    sink: &dyn DriftAlertSink,
) -> Result<usize, anyhow::Error> {
    let mut emitted = 0;
    for agg in aggregates {
        if !should_emit_drift_alert(agg, cfg) {
            continue;
        }
        let z = match compute_z_score(agg) {
            Some(z) => z,
            None => continue,
        };
        let mut ce = build_drift_alert(agg, z, cfg);
        // Sign the canonical bytes (matches tokenizer worker.rs
        // sign_in_place pattern).
        ce.signing_key_id = signer.key_id().to_string();
        ce.producer_id = signer.producer_identity().to_string();
        ce.producer_signature = bytes::Bytes::new();
        let canonical = ce.encode_to_vec();
        let sig = signer
            .sign(&canonical)
            .await
            .map_err(|e| anyhow::anyhow!("sign drift_alert CloudEvent: {e}"))?;
        ce.producer_signature = sig.bytes.into();

        match sink.emit(ce).await {
            Ok(()) => {
                emitted += 1;
                debug!(
                    tenant_id = %agg.tenant_id,
                    model = %agg.model,
                    agent_id = %agg.agent_id,
                    prompt_class = %agg.prompt_class,
                    z_score = z,
                    "drift alert emitted"
                );
            }
            Err(e) => {
                warn!(
                    tenant_id = %agg.tenant_id,
                    error = %e,
                    "drift alert emit failed; will retry next cycle"
                );
            }
        }
    }
    Ok(emitted)
}

 succeeded in 0ms:
    let mean_7d = agg.mean_7d?;
    let baseline_mean = agg.baseline_mean?;
    let baseline_stddev = agg.baseline_stddev?;
    if baseline_stddev <= 0.0 {
        // baseline_stddev == 0 → all baseline samples identical.
        // Strict spec interpretation: z-score is undefined → no alert.
        return None;
    }
    Some((mean_7d - baseline_mean) / baseline_stddev)
}

/// Decision predicate: should this bucket emit a drift alert?
pub fn should_emit_drift_alert(agg: &BucketAggregate, cfg: &DriftDetectorConfig) -> bool {
    let Some(z) = compute_z_score(agg) else {
        return false;
    };
    let Some(n7d) = agg.sample_size_7d else {
        return false;
    };
    z.abs() > cfg.drift_z_threshold && n7d >= cfg.min_samples_for_alert
}

/// Build (but do not sign or emit) the prediction_drift_alert
/// CloudEvent for a bucket. Separated from the emission step so unit
/// tests can verify the envelope shape without a tonic Channel.
pub fn build_drift_alert(
    agg: &BucketAggregate,
    z_score: f32,
    cfg: &DriftDetectorConfig,
) -> CloudEvent {
    use bytes::Bytes;
    let now = Utc::now();
    // R2 M2: payload reports baseline_* fields (window [now-30d, now-7d]
    // — distinct from mean_30d which is the strategy-B cache baseline).
    // Calibration-report consumers want both for trend analysis.
    let data = serde_json::json!({
        "tenant_id": agg.tenant_id.to_string(),
        "model": agg.model,
        "agent_id": agg.agent_id,
        "prompt_class": agg.prompt_class,
        "baseline_mean": agg.baseline_mean,
        "baseline_stddev": agg.baseline_stddev,
        "baseline_sample_size": agg.baseline_sample_size,
        "current_mean_7d": agg.mean_7d,
        "z_score": z_score,
        "sample_size_7d": agg.sample_size_7d,
        "sample_size_30d": agg.sample_size_30d,
        "drift_z_threshold": cfg.drift_z_threshold,
        "min_samples_for_alert": cfg.min_samples_for_alert,
        "suggested_action": suggest_action(z_score),
    });
    let data_bytes = serde_json::to_vec(&data).unwrap_or_default();

    CloudEvent {
        specversion: "1.0".to_string(),
        r#type: PREDICTION_DRIFT_ALERT_EVENT_TYPE.to_string(),
        source: format!("spendguard://stats-aggregator/{}", agg.tenant_id),
        id: uuid::Uuid::now_v7().to_string(),
        time: Some(prost_types::Timestamp {
            seconds: now.timestamp(),
            nanos: now.timestamp_subsec_nanos() as i32,
        }),
        datacontenttype: "application/json".to_string(),
        data: data_bytes.into(),
        tenant_id: agg.tenant_id.to_string(),
        producer_signature: Bytes::new(),
        ..Default::default()
    }
}

/// Sink trait — abstracts the actual canonical_ingest gRPC call so
/// tests can swap in an in-memory recorder.
#[async_trait::async_trait]
pub trait DriftAlertSink: Send + Sync {
    async fn emit(&self, event: CloudEvent) -> Result<(), anyhow::Error>;
}

/// Logging-only sink for demo / development. Logs the event at INFO
/// level instead of sending to canonical_ingest. Production Helm gate
/// rejects this path when chart.profile=production.
pub struct LoggingDriftAlertSink;

#[async_trait::async_trait]
impl DriftAlertSink for LoggingDriftAlertSink {
    async fn emit(&self, event: CloudEvent) -> Result<(), anyhow::Error> {
        info!(
            event_type = %event.r#type,
            event_id = %event.id,
            tenant_id = %event.tenant_id,
            "drift_alert emitted (log sink — demo only)"
        );

docs/slices/SLICE_01_canonical_events_migration.md:79:**Cross-DB migration ordering** (round-2 fix M16): ledger DB migrations MUST complete before canonical_ingest DB migrations. Reason: the canonical mirror columns assume the ledger side has already accepted them; the outbox_forwarder will not push rows whose ledger row failed to insert. The Helm chart's apply loop enforces this by processing the ledger glob before the canonical glob; out-of-band migration workflows must follow the same order manually. Within each DB the lexicographic file order is also load-bearing: 0046 (audit_outbox columns + trigger + TRUNCATE guard, atomic) MUST land before 0048 (tokenizer_versions + FK) so the FK target exists; 0013 (canonical_events mirror) MUST land before 0015 (quarantine table mirror — round-3 B2 added; round-2's 0014 schema_bundle placeholder was deleted in round-3 B3).
docs/slices/SLICE_01_canonical_events_migration.md:112:| schema_bundle_id rotation not coordinated | gradual rolling deployment | dual_read accept both old + new bundle_ids per `trace-schema-spec-v1alpha1.md` §6 dual_read |
docs/slices/SLICE_01_canonical_events_migration.md:169:5. What is the schema_bundle_id rotation plan? Does it coordinate with all 4 producer services (sidecar / webhook_receiver / ttl_sweeper / ledger invoice_reconcile)? **Round-3 answer (B3 reversal of round-2 M10)**: schema_bundle_id rotation is now **deferred to SLICE_06 producer slice**. Round-2 landed `0014_schema_bundle_prediction_v1alpha1.sql` with a placeholder hash + `cosign_verified_at = NULL`; both were security-hostile (the placeholder hash was reversible from a public string — `sha256("spendguard.v1alpha1+prediction")` — so any attacker holding the producer signing key could synthesise events that the placeholder bundle "verified", and the NULL cosign field meant nothing in code enforced "production must have cosign-verified bundles"). Round-3 DELETES 0014 + 0014_down. SLICE_01 ships the schema substrate only; producers MUST register a new **cosigned** bundle row BEFORE writing tag-300+ fields (the operator-side bundle builder per Trace §12 computes the real sha256 of canonicalized proto bytes + records cosign verification). canonical_ingest accepts both old and new bundles concurrently per Trace §6 dual_read.

docs/reviews/post-ga/POST_GA_06_stats_drift_hygiene/round-2-codex-review.txt:6864:- `services/stats_aggregator/src/drift_detector.rs:459` reserves the 24h cooldown before signing and before `sink.emit` succeeds. A transient signer/KMS/canonical_ingest failure means no immutable `prediction_drift_alert` exists, but future cycles suppress the real alert until cooldown expiry. This turns append failures into silent 24h alert loss; reserve only after durable append, or use a pending reservation that is cleared/expired on failure.

codex
**Blocker**
- None

**Major**
- None

**Minor**
- `services/stats_aggregator/src/drift_detector.rs:473`: Non-finite numeric guards are silently skipped before suppression accounting. `should_emit_drift_alert` returns false for NaN/Infinity/invalid thresholds, so `detect_and_emit` hits `continue` without incrementing `suppressed` or logging, despite `services/stats_aggregator/src/main.rs:287` claiming the suppressed metric covers numeric safety guards.

- `services/stats_aggregator/tests/cycle_e2e_postgres.rs:596`: The Postgres cooldown key test only varies `prompt_class` and `tenant_id`. The slice requires independent cooldown behavior across tenant/model/agent/prompt, but there is no coverage that changing `model` or `agent_id` bypasses an active cooldown.

- None

**Minor**
- `services/stats_aggregator/src/drift_detector.rs:473`: Non-finite numeric guards are silently skipped before suppression accounting. `should_emit_drift_alert` returns false for NaN/Infinity/invalid thresholds, so `detect_and_emit` hits `continue` without incrementing `suppressed` or logging, despite `services/stats_aggregator/src/main.rs:287` claiming the suppressed metric covers numeric safety guards.

- `services/stats_aggregator/tests/cycle_e2e_postgres.rs:596`: The Postgres cooldown key test only varies `prompt_class` and `tenant_id`. The slice requires independent cooldown behavior across tenant/model/agent/prompt, but there is no coverage that changing `model` or `agent_id` bypasses an active cooldown.
